  • Implied YoY growth vs the comparative figures cited in the market write-up: adjusted revenue +109.0% (£1.19bn vs £569.3m); adjusted operating profit +131.0% (£77.6m vs £33.6m). ()
  • Water: demand and momentum ahead of expectations, moving into year two of AMP8. ()
  • Rail: renewals volumes lower, but offset by increased demand for maintenance (notably over winter via reactive maintenance frameworks). ()
  • Infrastructure: trading resilient; positioning to capture opportunities from RIS3 commencing 01 April 2026. ()
  • Energy: expansion in electricity T&D “well received”; Emerald Power (acquired Oct 2025) and Excalon performing well into H2. ()
  • Civil Nuclear: Sellafield industrial action continues to weigh on performance at the site. ()
  • Onshore wind: Full Circle impacted by underperformance in French subsidiary; restructuring review underway. ()
  • M&A: assessing acquisitions in Environmental and Energy sectors; expects progress in H2 (active pipeline). ()
  • Order book: stated as record levels. ()
  • Company statement: “Underpinned by extensive Government spending commitments and long-term framework positions, the Group's order book continues to be at record levels, demonstrating our core capabilities and established presence in a diverse range of long-term, sustainable growth sectors. The Group remains strongly positioned with a well balanced portfolio of businesses, providing us with confidence in delivering against our full year expectations.” ()
  • Interim results date: 12 May 2026. ()

summary: Giorgi Gureshidze sold 1,000 Lion Finance shares at £93.65 per share
body:
Time Period Share sale executed 26 March 2026 (disclosed 31 March 2026) ()

  • PDMR: Giorgi Gureshidze (Head of Mass Retail, Bank of Georgia) sold 1,000 ordinary shares (GB00BF4HYT85) on London Stock Exchange (XLON). ()
  • Dealing price: £93.65 per share. ()
  • Gross proceeds: £93,646.70. ()

summary: Unilever to combine Foods business with McCormick in $44.8bn deal
body:
Time Period Deal announced 31 March 2026; targeted completion mid-2027 ()

  • Unilever confirmed it will combine its Foods business with McCormick; Unilever Foods implied enterprise value $44.8bn. ()
  • Consideration mix: Unilever + Unilever shareholders receive McCormick stock equating to 65.0% of fully diluted combined company equity (split: Unilever shareholders 55.1%, Unilever itself 9.9%) plus $15.7bn cash. ()
  • Unilever intends to sell down its retained 9.9% stake “not earlier than one year after closing” (lock-up period). ()
  • Valuation metrics disclosed by Unilever: EV/Sales 3.6x and EV/EBITDA 13.8x (based on McCormick 1-month VWAP referenced in the release). ()
  • Synergies: combined company expects ~$600m annual run-rate cost synergies (net of growth reinvestments) with “full value” targeted by end of year three; incremental cost/revenue synergies of $100m to be reinvested. ()
  • Cash usage framework (per Unilever): $15.7bn cash intended to offset one-off separation/tax costs, reduce leverage back to ~2.0x net debt/EBITDA post-close, and support €6bn share buybacks (2026–2029). ()
  • Unilever disclosed €400–500m stranded costs from separating Foods, plus €500m one-off restructuring costs (2027–2029) allocated to offset stranded costs; TSA expected for ~2 years post-close. ()
  • Market reaction described in your note: Unilever shares fell 7.3% on Tuesday and a further 2.1% on Wednesday (cumulative ≈ 9.2% over the two moves); market cap quoted at £89.93bn at 4,109p.
  • Barclays quote: “Despite the reaction, we like the deal structure. The 13.8x Ebitda multiple and cash component was better than expected and the stranded overheads outlined lower than expected. We liked the clarity on no transformational acquisitions. The prize of a pure play HPC [home and personal care] company will be worth it in the end.”
  • Barclays quote (concerns): “There seems to be unease regarding the complexity of the deal structure and particularly the equity component of the consideration,” Barclays said.
  • UBS quote (rating / transition risk): UBS reiterated ‘sell’ and said the deal leaves investors facing “a complex and uncertain 15 months ahead”.
  • UBS view (financial shape): UBS said it should improve underlying sales/volume growth, but reduce reported sales and operating profit by ~20% (roughly “a fifth”) and slightly dilute operating margins.
  • JPMorgan quote: “From a strategic standpoint, both deals make strong sense,” JPMorgan said…
  • JPMorgan quote (value creation hinge): “The bridge to value creation from the deal hinges on re-rating of the core HPC company,” JPMorgan said…
  • Barclays quote (strategy framing): “We don't subscribe to the 'desperation' narrative and think the move is a elegant solution,” the bank said, describing it as “a part of Unilever's long term strategy pivot.”
  • Barclays disclosure: remains overweight with 5,500p price target (implying ~31% upside vs the Tuesday close cited in your note).

summary: NatWest and Sainsbury's launch financial products via Sainsbury's digital channels in H2 2026
body:
Time Period Partnership announcement; products launching in H2 2026. ()

  • Sainsbury’s customers to access new NatWest-provided financial products (savings and loans) via Sainsbury’s digital channels. ()
  • Product rollout begins in the second half of 2026. ()
  • Nectar-specific proposition: “exclusive” benefits for Nectar members, including tailored rates on savings and loans. ()
  • New product: NatWest Nectar credit card (earn Nectar points on everyday purchases, with bonus-point opportunities). ()
  • New product: instant access savings. ()
  • New product: unsecured personal loans (distributed through Sainsbury’s digital channels). ()
  • Strategic context: follows NatWest’s 2025 acquisition of Sainsbury’s Bank’s core banking business (personal loans, credit cards, retail deposits). ()
  • Solange Chamberlain (NatWest) said the partnership combines “our expertise and financial knowhow with a seamless digital experience and Sainsbury's scale”. ()
  • Patrick Short (Sainsbury’s) said: “We're committed to working with trusted partners who share our values and dedication to delivering outstanding service for our customers. Building on our success together, we are pleased to extend our agreement with NatWest, whose deep expertise and customer-first approach continue to make them an ideal partner for our evolving financial services offering.” (

summary: IG Group launches £125m share buyback programme starting April 2026
body:
Time Period Buyback programme commencing 01 April 2026; first tranche expected to complete by 30 September 2026; strategic review outcome due autumn 2026. ()

  • Commenced new share buyback programme totalling up to £125m. ()
  • Tranche structure: first tranche up to £62.5m (50% of total programme), running 01 Apr 2026 – 30 Sep 2026; second tranche to be announced separately. ()
  • Execution: appointed Morgan Stanley to conduct the programme under pre-set parameters. ()
  • Stated purpose: reduce share capital (shares to be held in treasury per the RNS wording). ()
  • Buyback capacity: up to 36,155,787 shares available for repurchase under the relevant AGM authority referenced in the RNS. ()
  • Strategic review (announced 19 Mar 2026) to consider value-maximisation options including acquisitions, domicile/listing venues, and possible business combinations; outcome due autumn 2026. ()
  • Governance: FCA-approved Chair appointment means Andrew Barron assumes the role from 01 April 2026. ()

summary: LGI Homes opens Eldorado, its largest Seattle-area community with 491 homesites in Bremerton
body:
Time Period Community launch / grand opening announcement (Seattle-area) ()

  • Grand Opening of “Eldorado” in Bremerton; described as LGI’s largest Seattle-area community to date with 491 homesites. ()
  • Location positioning: Kitsap Peninsula, with access to Downtown Bremerton and Silverdale. ()
  • Quote (Zachary Penrod, VP Sales): “Eldorado is perfectly positioned for families, located just five minutes from Silverdale Waterfront Park and ten minutes from everything that Silverdale has to offer,” stated Zachary Penrod, Vice President of Sales. ()
  • Amenities: “more than ten acres of amenities,” valued at ~$3 million (walking trails, playground, recreational fields, picnic areas). ()
  • Product/pricing: 3/4/5-bedroom homes “starting in the low $500s.” ()
  • Quote (Penrod on spec/package): “We are so excited to offer our CompleteHome Plus™ package which includes desirable features like energy-efficient, stainless-steel Whirlpool® kitchen appliances, stunning quartz countertops, 42” upper cabinets, and recessed ENERGY STAR lighting. Stylish plank flooring, two-tone interior paint, and two-inch faux wood blinds throughout the home are a few more features that are all included in the sales price,” stated Penrod. ()
  • Quote (Penrod on military): “Thank you to all of the military members that we have met so far from the multiple bases close to Eldorado. We’re looking forward to continuing to support our active duty and veteran members yet to come,” stated Penrod. ()

summary: Ferrexpo faces critical liquidity crisis with only US$35m cash and US$80m VAT refunds withheld
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 trading/cash update (cash as at 31 March 2026); cash runway to end-June 2026; FY2025 results timing. ()

  • VAT refunds: Ukrainian authorities’ withholding continued in Q1; outstanding VAT refunds expected to be US$80m at end-March 2026. ()
  • Liquidity (31 Mar 2026): available cash US$35m; net cash ~US$25m (no debt except lease liabilities). ()
  • Liquidity (31 Mar 2026): net accessible cash ~US$22m excluding funds held with MBaer Merchant Bank. ()
  • Cash trajectory: available cash US$35m at 31 Mar 2026 vs US$47m at 31 Dec 2025 and US$30m at 27 Feb 2026. ()
  • Scale of VAT vs liquidity: VAT refunds due (~US$80m) are ~2.3x total available cash (US$35m) and ~3.6x net accessible cash (US$22m). ()
  • Runway: based on forecast cash burn, expects sufficient cash “until at least the end of June 2026.” ()
  • Funding: “actively exploring” options that could include an equity capital raise; “no certainty” of success. ()
  • Risk disclosure: “If the withholding of VAT refunds continues and funding issues are not resolved in sufficient time, this could give rise to material negative consequences for the Group”. ()
  • Operations: suspended in January 2026 after attacks on Ukrainian energy infrastructure; restarted February 2026; one pellet line operating. ()
  • Cost actions: reduced working time, procurement cuts, suspension of non-essential capex/overheads/CSR spend. ()
  • Reporting: needs more time to finalise FY2025 accounts; expects to report on or before 30 April 2026. ()

summary: Berkeley shifts focus to maximizing value from existing 50000-home landbank amid challenging market conditions
body:
Time Period Strategy update ahead of FY results (year ending 30 April 2026); guidance for FY26 and the four years to 2030. ()

  • Director dealing: Executive Chair Robert Perrins bought 7,000 shares at £28.78 (c. £201,482) after an unscheduled trading update drove a sharp sell-off. ()
  • FY26 guidance reiterated: expects pre-tax profit £450m (in line with guidance set two years ago) and ~£300m net cash. ()
  • Capital allocation shift: will not acquire new land while current conditions prevail, except through joint ventures; focus shifts to value creation from existing land holdings. ()
  • Existing land: holdings comprise 50,000+ homes, plus 10,000+ further pipeline homes (London & South-East). ()
  • Cost base: targeting 25% reduction in operating costs to £150m (implies a c. £200m prior run-rate on this metric). ()
  • Balance sheet / land creditors: targeting reduction to £470m from £900m (c. 48% reduction). ()
  • Medium-term profit outlook: expects to deliver £1.4bn+ pre-tax profit over the next four years to 2030 (c. £350m+ p.a., ~22% lower than the FY26 £450m level on an average annual basis). ()
  • Execution levers highlighted: tighter construction sequencing; flexing pace of BTR investment; disciplined capital allocation; continuing shareholder returns (with buybacks referenced as a tool when the share price is below NAV per share). ()

summary: easyGroup loses trademark case against Easyfeetstore; plans to appeal decision
body:
Time Period High Court trial in February 2026; judgment issued 01 April 2026 ()

  • easyGroup brought a trademark claim against Easyfeetstore (Estonia) and Easyfeet Inc (US) (orthopaedic/orthotic insoles), plus shareholder/director Andriy Klishyn. ()
  • Judge Richard Hacon dismissed the claim; found “easy” was the only similarity and there was “no likelihood” of confusion. ()
  • Judge: “I have found that there is no relevant similarity…” and (for six of seven trademarks) “no similarities in goods or services.” ()
  • Judge also found no “detriment” to easyGroup and no “unfair advantage”. ()
  • Context noted: easyGroup has 380+ “easy” branded ventures/websites (easyJet, easyBus, easyHotel etc.) and has pursued multiple “easy” trademark disputes. ()
  • Company response / appeal intent (spokesperson quote): “We strongly believe that this judgment is wrong because it undermines the ability of owners of famous families of brands, such as the easy family of brands, to protect consumers from confusion.

“The other side wants consumers to believe that they have something to do with the official easy family of brands when they do not.

“We will appeal and will overturn it, as we have done many times in the past.”

summary: AG Barr reports 18% profit growth and 11% dividend increase for FY2026
body:
Time Period Financial year ended 31 January 2026; director dealing executed Tuesday 31 March 2026 (per the director-dealings report). ()

  • Director dealing: CEO Euan Sutherland bought 11,956 shares at 662.44p (c. £79,201). ()
  • Statutory pre-tax profit: +18% to £62.6m (from £53.2m). ()
  • Revenue: +4.0% to £437.3m (from £420.4m). ()
  • Basic EPS: +18% to 42.27p (from 35.81p). ()
  • Adjusted profit before tax: £65.8m, +12.5% YoY (and marginally ahead of one cited analyst consensus point of £65.4m, i.e. ~0.6% beat). ()
  • Adjusted operating margin: +120 bps to 14.8%. ()
  • Dividends: total dividend 18.71p, +11% (from 16.86p); final dividend 15.27p. ()

summary: Great Portland Estates sells Regent Street property for £52m, achieving 10.9% IRR since 2009
body:
Time Period Disposal completed (announcement date 01 April 2026); asset held since December 2009; portfolio disposals measured vs March 2025 book values. ()

  • Great Ropemaker Partnership (50/50 JV managed by GPE) completed the sale of the 53-year short leasehold interest in 103/113 Regent Street, W1 to a private client of JLL. ()
  • Headline price: £52m, “marginally behind” March 2025 book value. ()
  • Pricing metrics: 7.2% net initial yield; £912 per sq ft capital value. ()
  • Asset: 56,850 sq ft, Grade II listed, Regent Street retail with offices above; fully let to UNIQLO (London flagship) until 2036. ()
  • Headlease: held leasehold from The Crown Estate, expiring April 2079. ()
  • Disposal roll-up: total sales in the last 12 months £490m, 2% ahead of March 2025 book values (implies ~£9.6m aggregate uplift vs book, based on the stated 2%). ()
  • Investment return: acquired December 2009; sale crystallises a whole-life ungeared IRR ~10.9%. ()
  • Sophie Pearce (Senior Portfolio Manager) said: “This sale marks a successful conclusion to a long-term investment for the Great Ropemaker Partnership. Since acquiring the building in 2009, we have benefited from strong occupier performance, sustained income growth and the enduring appeal of Regent Street as one of London's prime retail locations,” and “The transaction reflects our ongoing strategy to recycle capital into opportunities with greater potential for value creation, while delivering strong returns for our partners.” ()

summary: Credit Acceptance named 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces winner for sixth consecutive year
body:
Time Period N/A (workplace award announcement)

  • Named a 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces award winner for the sixth consecutive year.
  • Ranked #4 in the 1,000–2,499 employee size category (Top 10 again this year).
  • Also recognised in the Top Workplaces “Well-Being” specialty category.
  • Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde): “Being a Top Workplace reflects the commitment of our team members… and deliver on our mission of changing lives for dealers, consumers, and each other.”
  • Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde) on culture/remote work: “In a remote work environment like ours, it’s especially important that we remain intentional about connecting and collaborating across teams and living our PRIDE values…”
  • Operational detail: nearly 95% of team is fully remote.
  • Connectivity initiatives cited: annual Sales Leadership Exchange, Support and Operations Alignment Retreat, plus in-office days and regional roundtable meetings.
  • “Second workplace award… this year”: earlier in the month, reached #18 on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® list (highest-ever ranking for the company).
  • Other accolades listed over the past year include (non-exhaustive): Fortune Best Workplace for Financial Services and Insurance™, Best Workplace for Women™, People Magazine’s Companies that Care®, Top Workplace for Remote Work, and Best Place to Work in IT (Computerworld).
  • Methodology: Top Workplaces awards based on an Energage employee survey measuring “Workplace Experience Themes” linked to high performance.

summary: Germany reviews BP's planned Gelsenkirchen refinery sale to Klesch Group for regulatory approval
body: Time Period Proposed refinery disposal (completion targeted H2 2026) + regulatory review Germany’s Economy Ministry is reviewing BP’s planned sale of its Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group (per Der Spiegel). Ministry spokesperson declined to comment, citing trade/business secrets. Germany may prohibit acquisitions by non-European investors under certain conditions (context for the review). BP previously announced it had struck an agreement for Klesch Group to acquire the refinery; completion targeted second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval. Refinery detail: site annual processing capacity ~12 million tons of crude oil; primarily produces road and aviation transport fuels. Der Spiegel allegation: Klesch Group controlled via a network of subsidiaries registered in Jersey (described as a tax haven). Market reaction noted: BP shares +2.1% to 574.70p on the day mentioned, alongside higher Brent oil prices.

summary: Unilever acquires Gruns Nutrition, a gummy supplement brand, to strengthen premium wellness portfolio
body: Time Period Announced acquisition agreement (closing expected later in 2026) Announced an agreement to acquire Gruns Nutrition Inc (gummy-form supplements), as part of portfolio shift “towards premium and high growth spaces”. Product scope: Gruns provides nutrient-support gummies for adults and children. Unilever’s positioning of target + origin story: “Founded in 2023 by Chad Janis, Gruns has a clear mission to make daily nutrient support an easy, enjoyable ritual. Janis launched the company after recognising that the supplement industry's biggest barrier wasn't awareness, but adherence. In a short time, Gruns has become one of the largest brands in the US Greens Supplement category, building a digitally native, culture-driven brand that resonates with consumers,” Unilever said. Strategic framing quote: “This marks another step in optimising Unilever's portfolio towards premium and high growth spaces, with a stronger focus on Beauty & Wellbeing and the US market.” Deal terms: not disclosed. Expected closing: later this year (2026), subject to regulatory approvals. Market reaction noted: Unilever shares -1.0% to 4,254.50p on the day mentioned. Note: this item appears twice in the provided pack (Articles 26 and 27) with the same content.

summary: TechForce Robotics partners with Oncotelic to develop GMP-compliant AI-enabled pharmaceutical manufacturing robots
body: Time Period Broad industry commentary; references current regulatory shift + recent company initiatives Editorial frames EU GMP Annex 1 as updated guidance emphasising minimising human involvement in sterile production and encouraging barrier systems + automation to reduce contamination risk. Editorial claims FDA inspections continue to identify recurring compliance gaps (notably aseptic processing/documentation) and positions automation/data integrity as a growing compliance driver. Company-specific angle: TechForce Robotics described as building AI-enabled robotic platforms with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection aimed at regulated environments. Partnership noted: Nightfood/TechForce Robotics is said to be partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics to advance commercialisation of a PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform. Quote included in the piece: “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise. The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.” Larger peer-name “context” included by the editorial (presented as examples of the trend, not company filings): mentions claimed initiatives involving NVIDIA, Johnson & Johnson, Amazon/AWS, and Tesla in physical AI/robotics-enabled workflows.

summary: Nightfood's TechForce Robotics partners with Oncotelic to develop AI-enabled GMP-compliant pharmaceutical manufacturing robots
body:
Time Period Industry / regulatory backdrop + company positioning update (no explicit period stated)

  • Editorial frames pharma manufacturing as undergoing “structural transformation” driven by tighter contamination control, data integrity and traceability expectations (EU GMP Annex 1; FDA inspection findings).
  • Core positioning claim: TechForce Robotics is developing AI-enabled robotic platforms combining autonomous functionality with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection, aimed at “continuous” compliance in GMP-regulated environments.
  • The piece argues existing automation “has not fully addressed” persistent compliance gaps (aseptic processing, documentation), implying a need for more context-aware / adaptive robotics vs “predefined task” robots.
  • States Nightfood Holdings (via TechForce Robotics) is partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics on commercialising a “PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform.”
  • Full quote (collaboration milestone): “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise,” the announcement stated. “The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.”
  • Feed note: the same AINewsWire editorial appears multiple times in this file under different tickers (e.g., AMZN / NVDA contexts), but the profiled issuer content is essentially the same.

summary: Amazon Pharmacy launches Eli Lilly's GLP-1 obesity pill with same-day delivery expansion
body:
Time Period Product availability launch + delivery expansion (current; expansion “by year end”)

  • Amazon Pharmacy now offers Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 pill Foundayo™ (once-daily oral for adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related medical problems).
  • Pricing disclosed: with insurance “as low as $1 per day / $25 per month”; cash pay “$5 per day / $149 per month,” with manufacturer-sponsored coupons applied automatically pre-checkout.
  • Delivery footprint: Same-Day Delivery in “nearly 3,000 cities and towns,” expanding to “nearly 4,500 by year end” (implies +~1,500 locations / ~50% increase).
  • Service proposition vs mail-order: in remote areas, Amazon cites next-/two-/three-day options versus “five to 10 days” for other mail-order delivery services.
  • New channel: plans “soon” for in-office, licensed pharmacist-supported kiosks at select One Medical sites, enabling pickup “within minutes” after appointments.
  • Amazon Pharmacy quote (Tanvi Patel): “We are giving customers faster treatment options with our Same-Day Delivery offering, easy ways to save with automatically applied manufacturer coupons and the price transparency they need to make informed decisions about their care.”
  • Lilly quote (Laura Steele): “If prescribed, patients may be able to have Foundayo on their doorstep the same day or walk out of their doctor's office with it in-hand in Los Angeles — eliminating the waiting and uncertainty that patients may face when starting their treatment journey.”
  • Additional metrics claimed: Amazon Pharmacy says customers have saved “more than $200 million” through automatic coupons, with GLP-1 medications the largest savings category; it also notes GLP-1 delivery since 2021 and an existing collaboration with LillyDirect for insured customers.

summary: Citi upgrades Lloyds to buy, raising price target to 114p after 14% selloff
body:
Time Period Citi valuation view after a 14% sell-off since 2 February peak; outlook discussion through 2028

  • Citigroup upgraded Lloyds to ‘buy’ (from ‘neutral’) and raised price target to 114p (from 106p), a +7.5% increase in target.
  • Citi frames the setup after a 14% sell-off since the 2 February peak; says Lloyds now trades on 1.6x price-to-tangible book and 2027 P/E of 7.6x (“simply too cheap”).
  • Rate sensitivity angle: Citi sees Lloyds as a major beneficiary of higher reinvestment yields, partly due to longer average hedge duration vs peers, extending tailwinds into 2027 and 2028.
  • Non-NII: Citi forecasts +9% growth in non-net interest income in 2026.
  • Citi says the principal investor pushback is macro/political (UK outlook) rather than company-specific.
  • Political comments included: poor Labour local election showing “widely anticipated”; leadership change “remains unlikely”; UK fiscal position “better than feared” (better positioned for an energy shock).
  • Citi also notes: loan demand remains strong (esp. large corporates); unemployment rising but mainly among under-24s (small share of bank lending).
  • Market data included in note: current stock price 99.93p; 12-month change +55%.
  • Article appears twice in the pack (Articles 37 and 38) with the same content.

summary: BP discovers ~2 trillion cubic feet gas offshore Egypt with partner Eni
body:
Time Period New offshore Egypt gas discovery (reported “on Wednesday” with market move “on Thursday morning”)

  • BP reported a new gas deposit offshore Egypt (shared interest with Eni).
  • Discovery well: Denise W-1, part of the Temsah concession (Eastern Mediterranean).
  • Eni preliminary estimates: ~2 trillion cubic feet gas initially in place + 130 million barrels associated condensates.
  • Location: ~70km offshore, 95m water depth; <10km from existing infrastructure.
  • Reservoir quality/thickness: “High-quality gas-bearing sandstone reservoirs” across a ~90m gross interval.
  • BP positioning: says it’s BP’s second exploration discovery this year, following 12 discoveries in 2025.
  • Temsah concession: BP previously entered a binding agreement (July) with Egyptian state entities for a 20-year renewal.
  • Working interest: BP 50% and Eni 50%; Eni operates the Denise development lease via Petrobel (Eni/EGPC JV).
  • Other Egypt positions mentioned: BP took 100% of North-East El Alamein offshore concession and 25% of West El Hammad; also signed for Red Sea Block 6.
  • Market/context datapoints included: Brent referenced at $97.40/bbl vs $95.20 prior close; BP shares +2.3% to 575.78p (also +13% past month; +73% past year).
  • Corporate note included: appointment of new BP CEO Meg O’Neill and Deputy CEO Carol Howle referenced as occurring “last week”.

summary: Me Group partners with Asda to install up to 700 laundry machines across UK stores
body:
Time Period Partnership announcement; rollout target stated for financial 2026

  • New partnership with Asda Stores Ltd to install/operate laundry machines across Asda Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites in the UK.
  • Scale target: up to 700 laundry machines across these Asda locations (described as high-footfall).
  • Strategic linkage: ME says this supports its growth plan to install 1,300 laundry machines in financial 2026.
  • Long-term ambition stated: install >20,000 laundry machines globally.
  • Current footprint stated: >7,600 machines across 12 countries; >1,500 in the UK & Republic of Ireland.
  • Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “Asda is a perfect company to partner with, their retail and petrol stations are excellent locations for our services and their innovative and fast-paced approach will enable us to deploy our units rapidly.”
  • Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “We are delighted to announce this partnership; this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.”
  • Share price reaction in the piece: +3.7% to 147.00p (Thursday morning).

summary: Great Portland Estates fully lets SIX St Andrew Street office building to AI company
body:
Time Period Completion of a leasing campaign (at SIX St Andrew Street, Farringdon)

  • Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street (Farringdon, London) — 3rd and 4th floors, totaling >11,680 sq ft — to an AI company.
  • Building now fully occupied and generating >£8.8m annual rent at an average of £200/sq ft.
  • Letting metrics: rent level stated as 6.2% ahead of ERV and 6.2% yield on cost.
  • Portfolio mix datapoint: AI-related customers represent 11.7% of total office portfolio, or 25.2% of fully managed offices.
  • GPE quote: “This letting reflects the strong momentum in GPE's fully managed portfolio, where leasing activity accelerated during the final quarter of the financial year… with 19 deals completed and a further seven currently under offer.”
  • Molly Maguire (Senior Flex Leasing manager) quote: “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.”
  • Share price move in the piece: -0.9% to 305.80p (Thursday morning).

summary: Supermarket Income Reit declares third quarterly dividend of 1.545p per share, up 0.98%
body:
Time Period 1 January 2026 to 31 March 2026 (third quarterly dividend period) ()

  • Declared an interim dividend (“Third Quarterly Dividend”) of 1.545p per ordinary share. ()
  • Dividend increased vs prior-year comparable (1.53p → 1.545p): +0.015p / +0.98%. ()
  • Payment expected on or around 29 May 2026, as a Property Income Distribution. ()
  • Shareholders on the register as at 8 May 2026 will receive the dividend; ex-dividend date 7 May 2026. ()
  • No scrip dividend alternative available for this quarterly payout. ()

summary: LGI Homes launches Topaz at Skye Canyon, a 49-home Las Vegas townhome community starting at $380s
body:
Time Period N/A (community launch announcement)

  • Announced grand opening of Topaz at Skye Canyon, a new townhome community within the Skye Canyon development in Las Vegas.
  • Community size: 49 homesites.
  • Pricing: homes start from the “$380s.”
  • Product range: 3- and 4-bedroom townhome floor plans with attached two-car garages.
  • Four floor plans disclosed with sizes: 1,256 / 1,432 / 1,533 / 1,665 sq ft.
  • Includes “CompleteHome Plus™” upgrade package (e.g., energy-efficient Whirlpool stainless steel appliances, 42” wood cabinetry, tile backsplash, designer finishes).
  • Quote (Ron Christian, VP Operations – Nevada): “Expanding into Skye Canyon is an exciting opportunity for LGI Homes… It’s the perfect combination of new construction and a proven community setting where homeowners can feel at home from the very first day.”

summary: Blackbird integrates Epidemic Sound's royalty-free music library directly into its video editor platform
body: Time Period Current product launch (no financial period specified). Launched integrated royalty-free music from Epidemic Sound inside (Blackbird’s browser-based collaborative video editor). All users can now access Epidemic Sound’s catalogue directly within the editor (search/preview/add tracks without leaving the workflow). Licensing is handled at export, and can be tailored to user requirements. Chair Ian McDonough quote: “Music is fundamental to great storytelling, it's what gives video content its emotion and energy. So rather than sending creators off to find tracks elsewhere, we've built it straight into the platform. World-class music, right there in users' workflows. No interruptions, just creativity and complete peace of mind. This is a huge moment for the company and we're incredibly excited.”

summary: Diamond drilling program at Elizabeth Hill to test high-grade silver extensions below historic mine
body: Time Period Drilling early April 2026; assay results expected progressively through Q2 2026. JV partner West Coast Silver Ltd plans to start a 1,500-metre diamond drilling programme in early April at Elizabeth Hill Silver Project (near Karratha, Pilbara, Western Australia). Drill programme expected duration: 4–6 weeks. Assay results expected progressively throughout Q2. Drilling focus: test for extensions of mineralisation along strike of the historic Elizabeth Hill high-grade underground silver mine, plus structural targets along the Munni Munni Fault. Chair Bruce Garlick quote: “Our previous 2025 drilling confirmed that silver mineralisation at Elizabeth Hill extends beyond the historical underground mine and associated with a broader structural corridor along the Munni Munni Fault. This current program of diamond drilling below Elizabeth Hill is designed to determine whether there are high-grade silver extensions below and south of the mine. The drilling will also test whether high-grade mineralisation repeats at depth; a model that, if confirmed, could materially expand the resource potential.”

company: GreenTech Metals Ltd (JV partner update relevant to Alien Metals’ Munni Munni exposure)
summary: GreenTech identifies material upside potential at Munni Munni beyond historic resource estimate
body: Time Period Current exploration update (no financial period specified). GreenTech reports “material upside potential” at Munni Munni Platinum-Palladium-Copper-Nickel Project not captured in the historic mineral resource estimate. Historic MRE cited: 24 million tonnes @ 2.9 g/t (Pt, Pd, Rh & Au) for 2.2 million ounces total. Upside items highlighted: high-grade PGE zones above 4 g/t. Upside items highlighted: under-recognised copper and nickel mineralisation outside reef domains. Upside items highlighted: near-surface eastern mineralisation that may be suitable for open-pit mining.

summary: Chair opposes board changes at April 2026 shareholder meeting citing value creation risks
body: Time Period Ahead of requisitioned General Meeting on 29 April 2026. Chair Jim Millen urges shareholders to vote against the resolution to change the board composition at the requisitioned GM (29 April). Chair Jim Millen quote: “The board rejects the suggestion that the company has failed to create value. In addition to delivering specialist services to clients, the company has built valuable intellectual property (Virtual Tumour and our personalised dosing tool which is in development), proprietary know-how, modelling capability, scientific credibility and customer relationships that represent important strategic assets of the business,” Millen adds there is “a genuine risk” that disruption to current stewardship without continuity of relevant company/sector knowledge could impair the ability to commercialise the value created.

summary: IG Group plans to expand African mineral processing hubs and secure critical mineral supply agreements
body: Time Period Goals for the next 12 months. Targets launch of multiple processing hubs across Tanzania, Africa, and Latin America. Plans to scale mineral aggregation network via new artisanal & small-scale miners and semi-industrial partnerships. Intends to secure long-term agreements aligned with EU and US critical mineral requirements. Plans to progress ventures on high-scale assets confirmed through rapid validation protocols. Company comment quote: “The synergy between Amigo's visionary leadership and aggressive operational execution has created a platform of Tier-One potential. By controlling the processing nodes and the information layer of the African mineral economy, we are delivering a new standard of transparency and growth. We stand at a pivotal inflection point, uniquely positioned to capture the multi-billion-dollar industrial upside of African mineral beneficiation.”

summary: Crystal Amber Fund restarts share buyback programme for 2.7 million shares worth £3.0 million
body: Time Period Buyback restart (no financial period specified). Restarted share buyback programme. Intends to repurchase 2.7 million shares worth GBP3.0 million under the authorisation granted on 2 December. Notes there is no guarantee the programme will be implemented in full.

summary: Eden Research's February placing conditions satisfied, completing £7.6m fundraise at 4.0p per share
body: Time Period Placing conditions now satisfied (placing announced in February). Confirms all conditions relating to its conditional placing are now satisfied. February placing raised GBP7.6 million gross at 4.0p per share. Conditionality referenced: in part dependent on EIS/VCT legislative changes (announced in the UK Government Autumn Statement) passing into law.

summary: Quantum Helium begins extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well in Colorado
body: Time Period Extended production test to run “over the coming weeks”. Began an extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well (Colorado). Testing expected to run over the coming weeks; results to be announced after analysis is completed. CEO Howard McLaughlin quote: “Commencing the extended production test at Sagebrush-1 is a major milestone for Quantum and an important step forward for the company. This well has already demonstrated the presence of helium, and this programme is focused on confirming flow rates and commercial potential. The results will be critical in moving our prospective resources towards reserves and defining the pathway to development.”

summary: Guardian Metal Resources received notice of 300,000 share options exercise raising GBP42,000
body: Time Period Option exercise notice (no financial period specified). Received notice of the exercise of options over 300,000 shares, raising GBP42,000. Exercise price stated as 0.14p per share; note this appears arithmetically inconsistent with GBP42,000 proceeds (GBP42,000 over 300,000 shares implies 14p per share).

summary: CFO Nicholas Cadbury sold 225,000 IAG shares worth £815,175 on 2 April 2026
body: Time Period Director dealing dated 2 April 2026. On 2 April, CFO & Chief Sustainability Officer Nicholas Cadbury sold 225,000 shares at 362.3p, total GBP815,175.

summary: Martin Cooke steps down as director; Graham Oldroyd reverts to non-executive role
body: Time Period Board change effective 30 April 2026. Independent non-executive director Martin Cooke to step down 30 April. Deputy Chair Graham Oldroyd will revert to his former role as independent non-executive director. Chair Stephen Harris quote: “Given that we now have a stable platform for growth the board will be focusing on business opportunities and returning to a more common governance structure...We are actively pursuing the recruitment of a permanent chief executive officer and will update shareholders on progress with this search as soon as we have something able to.”

summary: Aurore Blair purchases 48,844 Trustpilot shares worth approximately £99,349
body: Time Period Dealing dated 2 April 2026. On 2 April, Aurore Blair (closely associated with CEO Adrian Blair) bought 48,844 shares at 203.4p average, total GBP99,349.

summary: Tullow Oil secured requisite consents for USD1.29bn 2026 notes restructuring
body: Time Period Consent solicitation for notes due 2026 (market data referenced “Wednesday afternoon”). Received requisite consents in a consent solicitation related to USD1.29bn 10.25% senior secured notes due 2026. Brent oil referenced at USD95.20/bbl (Wednesday afternoon) vs USD110.24/bbl around the same time the prior day (-13.6% day-on-day). Current stock price referenced: 12.46p, -3.7% on Wednesday; 12-month -8.9%.

summary: Wickes Group launches £10m share buyback programme set to conclude by end of 2026
body: Time Period Buyback runs until on or before 31 December 2026. Commenced a third share buyback programme worth up to GBP10.0m. Buyback will end on or before 31 December; purpose stated as reducing share capital with intent to cancel all repurchased shares. Appointed Investec Bank PLC to conduct the programme. Company can repurchase up to 16.5m shares, under authority granted at its AGM. Current stock price referenced: 222.10p, +6.0% on Wednesday; 12-month +25%.

summary: Jubilee Metals shareholders approved capital reduction to boost future dividends and buybacks
body: Time Period General Meeting outcome (reported “on Wednesday”). Shareholders approved proposed capital reduction plan. Rationale stated: restructure balance sheet to increase distributable reserves to support potential future dividends and share buybacks. Notes that a resolution to grant directors authority to allot shares was withdrawn. Share prices referenced: Johannesburg 74c (+4.2%) with 12-month -1.3%; London 3.23p (+1.1%) with 12-month +14%.

summary: Jubilee Metals Group shareholders approved capital reduction at April 2026 general meeting
body: Time Period General Meeting held 8 April 2026; capital reduction process timetable through 29 April 2026. GM held 8 April 2026; Resolution 1 (special resolution) to reduce the share premium account (the “Capital Reduction”) was passed. Voting: 98.42% of votes cast were in favour; votes cast represented 35.03% of the company’s total voting rights. Resolution 2 (authority to allot shares / grant rights to subscribe/convert) was withdrawn. Resolution 3 (dis-application of pre-emption rights for cash allotments) was withdrawn. Next step: company to apply to the Court for confirmation; capital reduction becomes effective only after Court confirmation and registration with the Registrar of Companies (England & Wales). Expected timetable: Court hearing 28 April 2026; Capital Reduction effective 29 April 2026.

summary: SLM Corporation will release Q1 2026 results on April 23 with webcast at 5:30 p.m. ET
body: Time Period Scheduled Q1 2026 results release and webcast (April 2026). Will release Q1 2026 financial results after market close on Thursday 23 April 2026. Webcast scheduled for Thursday 23 April 2026 at 5:30 p.m. ET. Live audio webcast and presentation slides to be available via ; investors advised to log in 15 minutes early. Earnings release and replay will be available on the investor site.

summary: Capricorn Energy's takeover deadline extended to May 6 2026 for bidder funding
body: Time Period Takeover Code “put up or shut up” deadline extended to 6 May 2026. Extended the deadline for Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC to announce a firm offer or walk away (per UK takeover rules). Previous deadline would have expired at 5pm London time “today”; new deadline set to 6 May. Extension rationale: gives the bidder (member of the Cafani Group) additional time to progress funding arrangements “as requested”. Capricorn says it has received multiple unsolicited takeover proposals. Capricorn had provided due diligence access earlier this year after evaluating multiple proposals. Share price reaction referenced: +3.2% to 325.00p on Wednesday afternoon.

summary: Rebecca Papp sold 4,033 Next shares worth £521,265 at £129.25 each
body:
Time Period Director/PCA dealing (announcement day)

  • Rebecca Papp (person closely associated with Group Merchandise & Operations Director Richard Papp) sold 4,033 shares at £129.25 each.
  • Total value of sale: £521,265.
  • “Current share price” cited: 13,305p (≈ £133.05), implying the cited market price is ~+2.9% above the disclosed sale price.
  • “12-month change” cited: +21%.

summary: Shell Q1 2026 guidance shows Integrated Gas weakness from Middle East conflict offsetting LNG and refining gains
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 trading update (guidance ranges)

  • Integrated Gas output expected 880k–920k boepd in Q1, down from 948k boepd in Q4 2025 (midpoint implies ~-5.1% QoQ).
  • Integrated Gas guidance reduced vs prior range 920k–980k boepd (new lower end -40k / ~-4.3% vs old lower end; new upper end -60k / ~-6.1% vs old upper end).
  • Shell attributes Integrated Gas weakness to “the impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes” and flags: “in light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.”
  • LNG volume guidance nudged up to 7.6–8.0mt from 7.4–8.0mt (lower end +0.2mt / ~+2.7%); Q4 LNG volumes were 7.8mt.
  • LNG drivers: “ramp-up of LNG Canada” offset by “Australia weather constraints” and “Qatar LNG outages.”
  • Upstream production expected 1.76–1.86m boepd, down from 1.89m boepd in Q4 (midpoint implies ~-4.2% QoQ); includes reduced production after the Adura JV incorporation.
  • Chemicals & Products indicative refining margin: $17/bbl vs $14/bbl in Q4 (+21.4% QoQ).
  • Trading statement language: “trading & optimisation is expected to be significantly higher” than Q4.
  • Balance sheet: non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3bn–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases.”
  • Working capital expected to “swing” to negative $10m–$15m in Q1 vs positive $1.3bn in the prior quarter (as stated in the article).
  • Market/analyst colour included in the piece: AJ Bell describes a “dichotomy” where higher energy prices help profits but Middle East footprint disrupts operations; JPMorgan flags a pricing vs balance sheet “timing mis-match” and a “heavy… working capital build.”
  • Share price / macro context cited: Shell shares -5.3% to 3,377.70p; Brent crude cited at $93.76/bbl vs $110.24 at prior London close (about -15.0%) after the US and Iran agreed a two-week ceasefire.

summary: Motorpoint reports record 65,000 retail volumes and £7.5m pretax profit, up 83% year-on-year
body:
Time Period FY ended 31 March 2026 (pre-close style update)

  • Retail volumes described as “record” 65,000 in FY26, +8.0% vs FY25.
  • Expects pretax profit of around £7.5m, up 83% vs £4.1m prior year (increase of ~£3.4m).
  • In calendar 2025, retail sales volumes +6.0%, vs overall used car market growth +2.2% (outperformance +3.8pp).
  • Company statement on AI impact: “Since launching our new agentic artificial intelligence tool to reactivate historic closed quotes, around 900 incremental vehicle sales can be attributed to this channel.”
  • Operational notes (per company statement): vehicle supply “more normalised”; “securing more bulk deals”; sourcing more vehicles via “Sell Your Car” channel; data-led buying/pricing “firmly embedded” and supported “record metal margin performance,” helping offset inflationary pressures (notably labour costs).
  • New store planned: Leeds opening “during the summer.”
  • CEO Mark Carpenter quote: “FY26 has been a step change year for Motorpoint, where the use of data became fundamental within the business and we started to embrace the benefits of AI. Whilst the macroeconomic uncertainty in recent weeks leads to a degree of caution due to the risks of increased inflation and interest rates, our superior customer service, omnichannel business model and exciting growth plans mean we are well placed to take advantage of opportunities to further increase market share and build long term value for shareholders.”
  • Annual results timing: to be released in June.
  • Share move cited: shares +4.6% to 136.00p.

summary: Close Brothers estimates £320m FCA motor finance redress cost, broadly in line with existing provisions
body:
Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme update (current)

  • FCA scheme scope cited: 12.1m agreements eligible vs 14.2m at consultation stage (reduction of 2.1m / ~-14.8%).
  • FCA scheme cost cited: ~£7.5bn compensation; ~£9.1bn total incl. admin (vs earlier projections up to £11bn).
  • Close Brothers estimates final scheme cost at around £320m, described as “broadly similar” to existing IAS 37 provision of £294m (delta +£26m / ~+8.8%).
  • Close Brothers language: cost can be “comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources, leaving it “well positioned” to keep “delivering its strategy.”
  • Provisioning status: “At this stage, no provision changes have been recognised. The group's existing IAS 37 provision remains under review,” and it will monitor further legal/regulatory/industry developments and consider next steps.
  • Capital impact: estimate would trim CET1 by ~25bps to 14.0% pro forma; remains above medium-term target 12%–13%.
  • UBS takeaways in the piece: outcome “reduces potential downside risks”; highlights that FirstRand increased provisions and that Viceroy Research argued Close Brothers would need to at least double provisions.
  • UBS sensitivity detail (as reported): the £320m estimate assumes 75% scheme take-up; each 5% change = £18m impact and 17bps CET1 impact.
  • Panmure Liberum scenario (as reported): assuming 100% take-up and excluding a £16m time value discount implies total cost around £426m, and the broker still expects sufficient CET1 headroom.
  • Shore Capital action (as reported): cut to ‘hold’ with “Take profits,” noting shares up 33% since its upgrade; warns “underlying operational performance remains weak” and “significant execution will be required.”
  • Share moves cited across the two wires: +16% to 451.54p in the longer “EXTRA” story; +18% to 457.94p in the shorter item.

summary: Greatland Resources delivered strong Q3 production with record cash balance and raised full-year guidance
body:
Time Period Quarter ended March 2026 (FY Q3) + YTD FY2026

  • Q3 production: 82,723 oz gold and 4,128 tonnes copper.
  • YTD production: 249,887 oz gold and 11.022 tonnes copper (as stated).
  • Q3 sales exceeded production: sold 97,800 oz gold and 4,620 tonnes copper (vs produced 82,723 oz / 4,128t).
  • YTD sales: 252,212 oz gold and 10.333 tonnes copper (as stated).
  • Cash build: “approximately $260m” in the quarter after capex and a $73m tax payment.
  • Balance sheet: $1.21bn cash and no debt at 31 March, up from $948m at end-2025 (increase $262m / ~+27.6%).
  • Guidance: now expects full-year production “around or slightly above” the upper end of prior guidance 260k–310k oz gold.
  • Operational note: Telfer not impacted by diesel supply disruptions linked to Middle East war because plant largely powered by onsite natural-gas generation; diesel used only for highest-grade ore sources.
  • Hedging note: retains full upside exposure to gold price with partial downside protection from put options.
  • Share move cited: +14% to 762.50p.

summary: RBC downgrades AB Foods to underperform, citing limited growth and Primark valuation concerns
body: Time Period Forward-looking (RBC forecast changes for FY2026–FY2028; expectation around ABF H1 results) RBC Capital Markets downgrades AB Foods to underperform (from sector perform). RBC cuts its price target to 1,850p (from 2,050p) (-9.8%). RBC sees further downside risk to consensus earnings, mainly driven by pressure on Primark. RBC quote (Primark / valuation): "Although we think a demerger should make ABF more investable in the long run, we think ABF's valuation is full given more limited growth prospects over the next few years," (Richard Chamberlain). RBC concern: Primark’s "value for money perception" could be damaged by attempts to elevate its offering. RBC highlights Primark’s relatively low gross margin, increasing sensitivity to rising cost pressures. RBC expects mixed performance in the Food arm this year. RBC notes Twinings has been "performing well". RBC notes Ovaltine has delivered "good results from marketing investments". RBC flags US pressure in cooking oils and bakery ingredients after a period of elevated pricing. RBC expects ABF to confirm plans to demerge Primark and Food at its first half results. RBC quote (growth outlook pre-demerger): "However, in advance of the demerger we think the growth outlook for both sides of the business looks challenging". RBC lowers its pretax profit forecasts for FY2026, FY2027 and FY2028 by 4%–7%. RBC says its revised forecasts are now 2%–10% below consensus. RBC lists upside risks including FX, a Sugar unit recovery, and further value-accretive M&A.

summary: Grove Court acquisition performing well with 97% occupancy and 5% rental uplift secured
body: Time Period Post-acquisition update (Grove Court acquired in February; current occupancy / rent review outcome) The recently acquired Grove Court portfolio has performed in line with expectations. Portfolio occupancy remains ~97%. A rent review at a motor dealership in Beaconsfield (Grove Court’s largest asset by income) delivered a 5% increase in passing rent. Custodian acquired the family-owned Grove Court portfolio in February for £35.9m. Richard Shepherd-Cross quote (portfolio rationale): "Following the strategic majority-share acquisition of Grove Court earlier this year, its complimentary properties have improved our existing portfolio by providing tenant diversification and further defensive income to help drive earnings, while helping us continue to add scale to our business," Richard Shepherd-Cross quote (uplift / latent value): "We have already secured a 5% rental uplift with the occupier at Grove Court’s largest asset by income, further cementing our belief that we can capture the latent value in this portfolio."

summary: UK government awards landmark contract to Rolls-Royce for three small modular reactors
body: Time Period Programme launch / next-stage development (contract signed; work starts immediately; milestones through to final investment decision) Government-owned GBE-N signed a “landmark contract” with Rolls-Royce SMR, enabling work to start immediately on three small modular reactors (SMRs). The UK government says the three SMRs should generate enough low-carbon electricity to power the equivalent of ~3 million homes. Alongside the contract, the National Wealth Fund plans to invest up to £599m to support Rolls-Royce SMR through next-stage design. Ministers describe this as the first formal contract for delivering SMRs in Europe. The project is expected to support ~3,000 jobs at peak construction, plus thousands more across the UK supply chain. Rolls-Royce SMR was selected as the preferred partner in June last year after a competitive process. The government allocated £2.6bn for the SMR programme. The government has said the first SMR nuclear power station will be built at Wylfa (North Wales) by GBE-N. Chancellor Rachel Reeves quote: "will strengthen our energy security, create skilled jobs and help to build a new generation of homegrown nuclear technology that will power our economy for decades to come". Rachel Reeves quote: "We have the right economic plan – one where growth and clean energy go hand-in-hand – and one that will benefit everyone across the country," The contract requires delivery against key milestones, starting with site-specific design, regulatory engagement, and planning processes, before a final decision on capital investment to proceed with construction. Energy Secretary Ed Miliband quote: "At a time of global instability, this is a major milestone for Britain's energy security." Ed Miliband quote: "We are backing a British company to deliver our first small modular reactors – creating a generation of good jobs, driving growth and providing clean, homegrown power for decades to come." Ed Miliband quote: "Our clean energy mission is the only route to getting off the rollercoaster of fossil fuels and take back control of our energy independence." GBE-N CEO Simon Roddy quote: "This agreement is a landmark moment for the nuclear industry." Simon Roddy quote: "Supporting skills, innovation and growing our industrial capability is essential to this partnership, and will ensure the UK is well-placed to deliver the next generation of nuclear infrastructure." GBE-N says it has already awarded over £350m of supply-chain contracts this year. National Wealth Fund CEO Oliver Holbourn quote: "significant moment for the future of our nuclear industry". Oliver Holbourn quote: "The development and deployment of Rolls-Royce SMR's technology here in the UK will create thousands of jobs and pave the way for an affordable, cleaner, and more secure energy system," Rolls-Royce SMR is the preferred SMR technology partner for Czech utility CEZ, with plans to provide up to 3GW of low-carbon energy in Czechia. Rolls-Royce SMR CEO Chris Cholerton quote: "This contract unlocks the delivery of our first three units at Wylfa and brings certainty to the UK SMR programme." Chris Cholerton quote: "With plans for up to six further units in Czechia, this announcement makes Rolls-Royce SMR the only company with multiple commitments in Europe."

summary: RBC downgrades WH Smith to sector perform citing UK and international outlook concerns
body: Time Period Forward-looking (RBC forecast changes for FY2026–FY2027; commentary on current trading drivers) RBC downgrades WH Smith to sector perform (from outperform). RBC cuts its price target to 650p (from 675p) (-3.7%). RBC lowers FY2026 and FY2027 pretax profit forecasts by 3%–4%, citing a more cautious view on the UK and Rest of World outlook (aligned to passenger-number expectations). RBC says its revised estimates now "sit just below" WH Smith’s FY2026 guidance. RBC expects UK pressure from a weaker employment outlook and higher air fares post Covid. RBC notes disruption from store closures at Heathrow Terminals 3–5. Separately, WH Smith confirms it is opening three new flagship stores at those Heathrow terminals. In the US, RBC expects Travel Essentials to continue performing well, supported by new store openings and higher passenger spend. RBC expects the group’s Rest of World focus to be on Australia, Ireland and Spain. RBC expects the profitability impact from the Middle East war to be "relatively immaterial". RBC quote: "Geopolitical risk aside, we think WH Smith still offers potential for an attractive longer term travel growth plus cash returns equity story, given its category mix management and operational/logistics skills, in what are captive markets," RBC adds WH Smith does not have surplus capital to return currently and is likely to prioritise balance sheet strengthening.

summary: Panmure Liberum downgrades Wickes to hold, cuts price target to 215p citing ambitious profit growth expectations
body: Time Period Forward-looking (Panmure Liberum view on consensus; forecast changes for FY2026–FY2027) Panmure Liberum downgrades Wickes to hold (from buy). Panmure Liberum cuts its price target to 215p (from 230p) (-6.5%). Panmure Liberum says consensus implies ~35% cumulative pretax profit growth to FY2027, which it views as too ambitious. Panmure Liberum quote: "Even under supportive assumptions, this would require significant annual productivity savings, which appears increasingly demanding given the efficiencies already delivered since FY22," Panmure Liberum sees upside pockets in Design & Installation and longer-term store rollout, but flags near-term execution risk. Panmure Liberum says TradePro growth appears to be maturing. Panmure Liberum says faster rollout of smaller-footprint stores is likely earnings dilutive and cash consumptive near term. Panmure Liberum cuts pretax profit forecasts by 7% for FY2026 and ~4% for FY2027. Panmure Liberum says this implies 18% cumulative pretax profit growth to FY2027 and puts it at the lower end of forecasts. Panmure Liberum quote: "In the absence of upgrades, and given downside risks we have identified, we downgrade our recommendation to hold,"

summary: LGI Homes launches Waters Farm community in Woodruff, South Carolina with homes starting in the $270s
body: Time Period Community launch / sales offer (pricing “starting in the $270s”; full build-out plan) LGI Homes announces Waters Farm, a new single-family community in Woodruff, South Carolina. Location positioning: short drive to Greenville and Spartanburg; commute access to employers including BMW Manufacturing, GE, and Michelin. Homes: three- and four-bedroom single-family across six floor plans, with four designs new to the Greenville market. Included “CompleteHome™” upgrades at no additional cost: energy-efficient Whirlpool® appliances, granite countertops, luxury vinyl plank flooring, recessed lighting, landscaped front yards. Other included features: two-car garages, open-concept layouts, builder-paid closing costs. Quote (Chris Cositore): “Waters Farm offers the perfect balance of community, comfort and accessibility,” Quote (Chris Cositore): “Homebuyers will enjoy the charm of downtown Woodruff paired with quick access to Greenville and Spartanburg, along with professionally designed homes that are ready to live in from day one.” Floor plans (sq ft): Arcadia 1,309; Harris 1,415; Carolina 1,700; Ashwood 2,060; Piedmont 1,886; Cherokee 2,320. Amenities: onsite children’s playground; proximity to McKinney Park, Woodruff Greenway, Croft State Park. Full build-out: 198 homesites. Pricing: starting in the $270s. Sales center: 112 Whiteheath Court, Woodruff, SC 29388; open daily; phone (877) 691-8369.

summary: BP expects exceptional Q1 2025 results driven by higher oil prices and refining margins
body:
Time Period Q1 2025 (three months to March 2025)

  • Expects an “exceptional” oil trading result in Q1 2025 after a “weak” Q4.
  • Brent crude average: USD81.13/bbl vs USD63.73/bbl in Q4 (+27%).
  • Refining indicator margin: USD16.9/bbl vs USD15.2/bbl (+11%).
  • Flagged “heightened volatility” in crude, gas and refined products due to the Middle East crisis, expected to impact results (including trading results and working capital movements).
  • Upstream production expected broadly flat vs Q4 (2,344 mboe/d); gas & low carbon slightly higher; oil slightly lower vs 1,555 mboe/d.
  • Gas & low carbon: realisations expected broadly flat; gas marketing & trading result expected to be “average”.
  • Customers & products: seasonally lower volumes and lower retail fuels margins, more than offset by stronger midstream performance and stronger realised refining margins.
  • Net debt expected USD25–27bn vs USD22.2bn at end-Q4 (+13% to +22%).
  • Net debt increase driven primarily by working capital build of USD4–7bn, “largely due to the price environment.”
  • Capex expected broadly flat vs USD3.5bn in the prior quarter.

company: Schroder European Real Estate Investment Trust Ltd
summary: Portfolio value declined 0.7% to EUR192.6m despite strong performance from Rumilly and Stuttgart
body:
Time Period Quarter ended 31 March 2025

  • Portfolio independently valued at EUR192.6m vs EUR194.0m at 31 Dec (-0.7% QoQ).
  • Valuation at 31 March 2025 also cited as EUR194.0m (implies -0.7% vs that comparator).
  • Rumilly and Stuttgart delivered strong valuation growth attributed to lease extensions.
  • Tenant-related setbacks impacted other assets, particularly Alkmaar and Cannes.

summary: MAB acquires HomeOwners Alliance for £1.4m to expand home-buying services
body:
Time Period Acquisition announcement (no reporting period stated)

  • Acquired HomeOwners Alliance Ltd (HOA) to engage customers earlier in the home-buying process.
  • Total consideration GBP1.4m, plus distributable reserves.
  • Payment terms: 50% on completion; remaining 50% payable over two years.
  • Acquired the full share capital from the founders and Smoove Ltd.
  • Rationale: strengthens positioning ahead of potential changes from the government’s ongoing home-selling and buying consultation.
  • Expects integration/enhancement of HOA’s digital platform to increase lead volumes and extend the range of services.
  • Expects to strengthen connections between mortgage advice and complementary home-moving services.
  • Ben Thompson (Director of Home Moving Strategy) said:

“HomeOwners Alliance has built a strong reputation as a trusted source of information for homeowners and prospective buyers,” “This acquisition strengthens our ability to engage with customers earlier in their homebuying journey and supports our strategy to broaden MAB's role across the wider home-moving process. We're pleased to welcome HomeOwners Alliance to the Group and look forward to supporting its continued growth.”

summary: Morgan Stanley downgrades UK housebuilders citing macro risks from Middle East conflict and rate pressures
body:
Time Period Analyst note (macro/rates outlook)

  • Warned UK housebuilders face rising “macro risk” as the Middle East conflict “alters the industry backdrop materially.”
  • Transmission mechanism cited: higher oil/energy prices → higher swap rates → reduced expectations for Bank of England cuts → weaker housing demand.
  • Analysts said: “Valuation support alone has repeatedly failed to act as a catalyst in this cycle.”
  • Downgraded Barratt Redrow to equal-weight from overweight; cut price target to 300p from 430p (-30%).
  • Downgraded Berkeley Group to underweight from equal-weight; cut price target to 2,990p from 3,840p (-22%).
  • Price target cuts: Persimmon 1,390p from 1,610p (-14%); Taylor Wimpey 80p from 90p (-11%); Vistry 380p from 510p (-26%).
  • Ratings: Persimmon overweight (housebuilding preference), Vistry equal-weight, Taylor Wimpey underweight.
  • On Berkeley: cited “cut profit forecasts, paused land acquisition and prioritised balance sheet strength over growth amid weak London and South East demand,” and referenced re-phasing ‘Berkeley 2035’ delivery over four years to 2030.
  • On Barratt Redrow: “scale and a multi-brand platform offer strategic flexibility” but revenue synergies constrained in weak demand; performance increasingly macro-driven.
  • On Persimmon: viewed as “more appealing” and “the cleanest way to gain exposure to a volume-led recovery, should rate conditions ease,” citing above-average margins/returns and limited exposure to London.

summary: Taseko's Florence copper mine began production while Gibraltar significantly increased output year-over-year
body:
Time Period First quarter production update (comparatives provided vs Q1 2025)

  • Florence Copper SX/EW plant commenced operations mid-February; first copper cathodes harvested end-February.
  • Florence Copper Q1 production: 1.5m lbs copper cathode.
  • Solutions flowing in the wellfield since late 2025; initial leaching/production in line with expectations based on modelling.
  • Operational focus: balancing solution flow and grades from the wellfield through to cathode production.
  • CEO Stuart McDonald said:

“We are happy with the results from the first months of in-situ copper production at Florence. With the successful start-up behind us, the focus is turning to the production ramp-up. Additional newly constructed wells are now being integrated into the system, which will allow for higher solution flows and copper production in the coming weeks. Wellfield expansion continues, with four drills currently operating and a fifth expected to commence shortly.”

  • Gibraltar Q1 production: 30.0m lbs copper and 717k lbs molybdenum.
  • YoY vs Q1 2025: copper +50%; molybdenum +113%.
  • Recoveries improved to 83%; copper grades in line with life-of-mine average.
  • Copper sales: 27m lbs, slightly below production due to shipment timing.
  • Gibraltar SX/EW plant produced 733k lbs copper cathode and operated continuously through winter.
  • Average LME copper price in Q1 was +16% vs previous quarter.
  • Diesel price increases could raise Gibraltar operating costs by US$0.10–US$0.15/lb this year.
  • Florence has a fixed price contract for all sulphuric acid requirements in 2026; expects no impact from recent inflation/supply chain issues.
  • Outlook statement: continues to anticipate strong financial performance from steady Gibraltar production and growing Florence production.

summary: NVIDIA launches Ising, open source quantum AI models for processor calibration and error correction
body:
Time Period Product announcement (no reporting period stated)

  • Announced NVIDIA Ising, described as “the world’s first family of open source quantum AI models” for quantum processor calibration and quantum error correction.
  • Claims: up to 2.5x faster performance and 3x higher accuracy for QEC decoding vs pyMatching.
  • Jensen Huang said: “AI is essential to making quantum computing practical… With Ising, AI becomes the control plane — the operating system of quantum machines — transforming fragile qubits to scalable and reliable quantum-GPU systems.”
  • Ising Calibration: vision language model to interpret/react to measurements; aims to reduce calibration time from days to hours.
  • Ising Decoding: two variants of a 3D CNN optimised for speed or accuracy for real-time QEC decoding.
  • Adoption claims: Calibration already in use by multiple enterprises and labs (incl. UK National Physical Laboratory); Decoding deployed by multiple universities and labs.
  • Includes workflow “cookbook,” training data, and NVIDIA NIM™ microservices; models can run locally.
  • Complements CUDA-Q™ and integrates with NVQLink™ for real-time control and error correction.
  • Market sizing cited: quantum computing market expected to surpass $11bn in 2030 (Resonance).
