  • Berkeley Group rating cut to Underweight (from Equal-weight).
  • Berkeley price target cut to 2,990p from 3,840p (-22.1%).
  • Berkeley share move noted: -0.6% to 3,430.0p (Tuesday afternoon).
  • Berkeley rationale: has cut profit forecasts, paused land acquisition, and is prioritising balance sheet strength over growth amid weak London/South East demand; re-phasing “Berkeley 2035” delivery to 2030 referenced.
  • Persimmon price target cut to 1,390p from 1,610p (-13.7%) while retaining Overweight and described as housebuilding preference.
  • Persimmon share move noted: +0.3% to 1,144.0p (Tuesday afternoon).
  • Persimmon rationale: “cleanest” exposure to a volume-led recovery if rates ease; highlighted above-average margins/returns and limited London exposure.
  • Taylor Wimpey price target cut to 80p from 90p (-11.1%) and rated Underweight.
  • Taylor Wimpey share move noted: +0.1% to 85.14p (Tuesday afternoon).
  • Vistry price target cut to 380p from 510p (-25.5%) and rated Equal weight.
  • Vistry share move noted: +1.8% to 332.6p (Tuesday afternoon).

summary: CME Group launching options on Eris SOFR Swap futures June 2026 for interest rate risk management
body:
Time Period Announcement (product launch planned)

  • Will launch options on Eris SOFR Swap futures on 16 June 2026, pending regulatory review.
  • Options will be listed on 2-year, 5-year and 10-year Eris SOFR Swap futures (aimed at more sophisticated hedging / non-linear risk).
  • Stated use-case: help manage U.S. dollar interest rate risk, including non-linear risk in mortgage-backed portfolios.
  • “Since launching in October 2020, more than 10 million Eris SOFR Swap futures contracts have traded at CME Group.”
  • “In March 2026, Eris SOFR Swap futures reached an all-time open interest record of 707,000 contracts ($71B notional), including a single-day volume record of 299,513 contracts on March 10.”
  • Options will be eligible for margin offsets with other cleared interest rate futures/options at CME Group; products listed by / subject to the rules of CBOT.
  • Agha Mirza quote: “Our new options on Eris SOFR Swap futures will provide clients with additional flexibility in managing U.S. dollar interest rate risk…”
  • Michael Riddle quote: “Eris SOFR Swap options will help institutional investors manage risk with greater precision… By mirroring the structure of forward-premium OTC swaptions…”
  • Don Wilson quote: “CME Group's addition of options will enable more sophisticated risk management strategies while preserving the capital and liquidity advantages…”
  • Jeff Bauman quote: “Eris SOFR Swap options will enable clients to better manage the convexity of their portfolios, while providing a flexible, capital-efficient extension…”

summary: Florence Copper commenced operations with strong Q1 production; Gibraltar significantly exceeded prior year output
body:
Time Period Q1 2026

  • Florence Copper SX/EW plant commenced operations mid-February 2026; first copper cathodes harvested end-February 2026.
  • Florence Copper Q1 production: 1.5 million pounds of copper cathode produced.
  • Solutions flowing in the wellfield since late 2025; initial leaching/production described as in line with modelling/expectations.
  • Operational focus recently: balancing solution flow and grades from wellfield through to cathode production.
  • Stuart McDonald quote: “We are happy with the results from the first months of in-situ copper production at Florence… the focus is turning to the production ramp-up…”
  • Ramp-up / expansion: additional newly constructed wells being integrated; wellfield expansion continues with four drills operating and a fifth expected to commence shortly.
  • Gibraltar Mine Q1 production: 30.0 million pounds copper and 717 thousand pounds molybdenum.
  • Gibraltar YoY production change vs Q1 2025: copper +50%, molybdenum +113%.
  • Gibraltar: copper grades in line with life-of-mine average; recoveries improved to 83%.
  • Gibraltar Q1 copper sales: 27 million pounds, slightly lower than production due to shipment timing.
  • Gibraltar SX/EW plant produced 733 thousand pounds of copper cathode and operated continuously through winter months.
  • Market/cost commentary: average LME copper price in Q1 was +16% vs previous quarter.
  • Cost sensitivity: diesel prices at current levels would raise Gibraltar operating costs by approximately US$0.10–0.15/lb this year.
  • Input hedging: Florence has a fixed price contract for all sulphuric acid requirements in 2026, so expects no impact from recent inflation/supply-chain issues.
  • McDonald quote (outlook): “Overall, we continue to anticipate strong financial performance from steady Gibraltar production and growing production from Florence Copper.”

summary: Steve Mogford appointed Chairman of Intertek Group plc from May 2026
body:
Time Period Announcement (board / external appointment)

  • Steve Mogford (Costain Non-Executive Director) will be appointed Chairman of Intertek Group plc following Intertek’s AGM on 20 May 2026.

summary: Taseko Q1 2026 results show strong copper production with Florence SX/EW ramp-up progressing
body:
Time Period Q1 2026

  • Market reaction noted: shares closed +3.7% at 549.5p (London, Tuesday).
  • Recaps the same operational/production disclosures: Florence SX/EW start-up timing, 1.5m lbs Q1 cathode; Gibraltar 30.0m lbs copper and 717k lbs molybdenum; recoveries 83%; Q1 sales 27m lbs; diesel sensitivity US$0.10–0.15/lb; sulphuric acid fixed-price contract for 2026; and the CEO quotes on ramp-up/financial outlook.

summary: Next explores acquisition of struggling British handbag brand Radley alongside Gordon Brothers
body:
Time Period Announcement / media report (no financial period for Next; Radley FY figures disclosed)

  • Sky News-reported: Next is weighing a takeover of Radley (British handbag brand).
  • Structure discussed: potential deal would involve acquiring brand and IP assets (not necessarily the whole operating company).
  • Sale-process dynamic: industry sources suggested it might be implemented via a pre-pack administration, with outcome “some way” from being determined.
  • Competitive context: Next is one of the suitors alongside turnaround investor Gordon Brothers (per report).
  • Positioning: would be another small “bolt-on” acquisition for Next; article cites recent Russell & Bromley purchase and prior brand deals (Cath Kidston, Joules, Seraphine).
  • Radley FY to April 2025: reported loss of GBP2.2m.
  • Radley revenue: GBP65.8m, down from GBP72.0m (YoY -8.6%).
  • Radley geographic mix: the US ~15% of group revenues (per report).

summary: Adam Daniels appointed Chief Executive Officer of Vistry Group PLC effective immediately
body:
Time Period N/A (board/leadership change; effective immediately)

  • Adam Daniels appointed Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director with immediate effect (previously Executive Chair of one of Vistry’s two largest operating divisions; member of the Group’s Executive Leadership Team).
  • Background: joined Countryside Partnerships in 2016; first senior leadership role in 2019; previously worked at Galliford Try Partnerships and Bloor Homes.
  • Board states appointment follows a “thorough multi-year CEO succession and development process” led by the Nomination Committee (internal + external candidates considered).
  • Rob Woodward (Non-Executive Chair) quote: “I am delighted to confirm the appointment of Adam Daniels as CEO of Vistry. Adam is a proven leader, clear thinker and strong operator. I am looking forward to working closely with Adam, our CFO Tim Lawlor, and the rest of the Executive Leadership Team to ensure we maximise the strengths and opportunities of the Group's mixed-tenure Partnerships model. The Board is confident that Adam's collaborative, values-led approach will leverage Vistry's unique partnerships capabilities, resources and highly talented people to deliver on the Group's clear potential and create long-term value for all stakeholders.”
  • Adam Daniels quote (positioning and internal priorities): “I have been privileged to lead many parts of the organisation, and I am now honoured to be appointed CEO. I see first-hand the pride and passion that our people bring in delivering the high quality, mixed-tenure homes this country so badly needs.”
  • Adam Daniels quote (2026 operational focus): “As outlined at the time of our 2025 results last month, the Group is currently focused on improving cash generation, driving Open Market sales and reducing inventory levels. These objectives remain an absolute priority for Tim and I during 2026 to ensure the business is well positioned to capture the opportunities ahead.”
  • Adam Daniels quote (strategic framing): “Vistry is a great business, with an unrivalled position in leading the industry in the delivery of Affordable Housing, and a strong track record of delivery in the homebuilding sector. We have a unique Partnerships model, strong relationships with our partners, and the benefits of our approach will be central to realising our clear potential and creating long-term value.”
  • Greg Fitzgerald (by mutual agreement) steps down as Executive Chair and CEO and as a Board Director with immediate effect; remains available to assist transition.
  • Governance changes: Rob Woodward takes up role as Chair; Rowan Baker becomes Senior Independent Director (both with immediate effect).
  • AGM mechanics: resolution to re-elect Greg Fitzgerald as a Director (resolution number 5) withdrawn; all other AGM resolutions remain unchanged; proxies already submitted for other resolutions remain valid.

summary: CREI completes Grove Court acquisition with 97% occupancy and secures 5% rent increase
body:
Time Period Update since completion of Grove Court Transaction (announced 16 February 2026)

  • Provides update on majority-share acquisition of Grove Court Properties (Holdings) Limited, delivering a £35.9m family-owned property portfolio (“Grove Court Portfolio”).
  • Post-completion performance: occupancy c. 97%, described as in line with expectations.
  • Rent review completed at the Grove Court Portfolio’s largest asset/occupier by income (motor dealership in Beaconsfield): +5% increase in passing rent, stated to be in line with estimated rental value.
  • After the rent review, the Beaconsfield motor dealership now accounts for 27% of the portfolio’s rent roll.
  • Deferred consideration settled relating to a performance-related overage payment, and issuance of the final tranche of equity consideration (following rent review and finalisation of completion accounts).
  • Investment Manager quote (Richard Shepherd-Cross): “Following the strategic majority-share acquisition of Grove Court earlier this year, its complimentary properties have improved our existing portfolio by providing tenant diversification and further defensive income to help drive earnings, while helping us continue to add scale to our business. We have already secured a 5% rental uplift with the occupier at Grove Court’s largest asset by income, further cementing our belief that we can capture the latent value in this portfolio.”
  • Richard Shepherd-Cross quote (strategy + consolidation): “Despite macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty, this innovative growth strategy has proved to be an effective way to continue scaling our diversified portfolio, while also benefitting our income focused strategy. In a short space of time, we have undertaken three separate corporate acquisitions of family held property holdings totalling c.£66.5m, demonstrating our commitment to generating shareholder value through disciplined consolidation. We recognise the importance of growing the business alongside earnings, and creating value for shareholders is front of mind, as we continue to progress our pipeline of similar opportunities.”
  • Transaction consideration at completion (February 2026): £9.0m cash + 24,116,729 new ordinary shares.
  • Additional consideration components (expected at completion): 0.8m further new shares on finalisation of completion accounts; £0.3m cash overage linked to rent review (£0.2m) and lease renewal (£0.1m).
  • Today’s update on overage: confirms payment of £0.2m overage following successful completion of the rent review (in line with expectations) — implying the £0.1m lease-renewal-linked overage remains outstanding/conditional.
  • Today’s update on equity issuance: completion accounts finalised; 735,920 new ordinary shares to be admitted to LSE Main Market (premium segment); Admission expected 8am on 16 April 2026; new shares rank pari passu.
  • Potential further equity: may issue further new shares to settle acquisition retentions up to £0.6m maximum, subject to outcome of an upcoming lease expiry and completion of certain capital works.

summary: Sirius Real Estate reports 18.4% rent roll growth despite UK market uncertainty
body:
Time Period Financial year ended 31 March 2026 (trading update)

  • Group rent roll up 18.4% year-on-year (reported on constant FX basis GBP:EUR 1.152, using closing FX at 31 March 2026).
  • Like-for-like rent roll up 6.4% year-on-year (twelfth consecutive year of >5% like-for-like rent roll growth).
  • Company expects full year results to be in line with market expectations.
  • Germany: first-half move-outs more than offset by pricing gains on renewals and increased occupier activity (especially in Q4); expects income growth to convert into valuation growth at year end, with stable property yields.
  • U.K.: performance impacted by prolonged political uncertainty around the Chancellor’s Autumn Statement delayed to end-November 2025; Q4 calendar 2025 slower occupational market; post-statement, start of 2026 “very strongly” with signs of catch-up.
  • U.K. customer behaviour noted: deferred decision-making; sale of smaller spaces; occupiers reducing propensity to plan for future growth ambitions.
  • Group valuation outlook: expects to maintain U.K. valuations and announce positive valuation movement at Group level at period end.
  • Acquisitions: completed 13 asset acquisitions in the year with total investment value €464m.
  • Defence-related exposure in acquisitions: 3 assets (Bedford, Feldkirchen, Kiel) totalling ~€155m investment value, with a significant defence component to the tenant base (≈33.4% of the €464m acquisition spend).
  • Macro/strategy framing: cites both Germany and the U.K. announcing material increases in defence spend; highlights Germany targeting 5% of GDP with committed fiscal stimulus ~€400bn; expects funding to drive demand for the industrial space Sirius provides (with “existing stock” the only feasible option at scale).
  • Balance sheet: renewed and enlarged €300m revolving credit facility, supported by existing and new banking partners.
  • Equity raise: February 2026 £77m fundraise at a price in line with 30 September 2025 adjusted NAV, intended to fund Kiel + one additional defence-related asset (combined ~€130m).
  • Kiel acquisition: completed at €93.4m (slightly improved price) within six weeks of the fundraise and before start of the new financial year; represents ~75% of the originally targeted spend.
  • Second targeted asset: withdrawn from due to seller significantly increasing price expectations.
  • Replacement pipeline: identified two alternative assets totalling ~€30m (remaining ~25% of targeted spend), one defence-related.
  • Forward acquisition timing: expects to notarise the two acquisitions in Q1 of the new financial year and complete in Q2, at a better blended yield than the 7.6% EPRA net initial yield that Kiel plus the withdrawn asset would have delivered.
  • Capital recycling: agreed sale of Pfungstadt for €30m, at a premium to book value, due to complete July 2026.
  • Additional disposal: sold smaller asset in Sunderland for £1.25m, also at a premium to book value.
  • CEO quote (Andrew Coombs): “Over the past year, our focus on growth and asset management, as well as the quality and occupier appeal of our properties, has enabled us once again to deliver a strong performance on behalf of our shareholders, despite the volatile market backdrop. This has translated into our twelfth year of like-for-like rental growth of above 5%, as well as improving occupancy and expected overall valuations. Our balance sheet remains strong and having shown that the debt and equity markets will continue to support our strategy when we seek new capital, we invested €464 million into primarily resilient, income-generating assets that also offer the opportunity to create value through our platform. Around a third of this by value was invested into business parks that bolster our portfolio of industrial assets let to defence-related businesses, which we expect to continue to benefit from increased government led defence spending across Europe and further afield.”
  • Full year results date: Monday, 1 June 2026 (in-person presentation + virtual webinar).
  • Note: financial information in trading update not reviewed/reported on by external auditors or a reporting accountant.
  • Metric caveat: excludes Vantage Point (U.K.) from like-for-like rent roll metrics due to ongoing improvement works.

summary: Hill & Smith completes acquisition of Freeberg Industrial Fabrication Corp
body:
Time Period N/A (transaction completion announcement)

  • Announced completion of the acquisition of Freeberg Industrial Fabrication Corp., on the terms previously announced.
  • No additional financial terms, synergy targets, or integration timeline provided in this release beyond completion confirmation.

summary: BP acquires 60% interest in three Namibian offshore oil licenses from Eco Atlantic
body:
Time Period Transaction announcement (agreement signed “Friday” prior to 13 April 2026)

  • BP to acquire 60% interest and become operator of three offshore Namibian licences in Walvis Basin: PEL97 (Cooper), PEL99 (Guy), PEL100 (Tamar).
  • Eco Atlantic disclosed a USD 2.7m one-time cash consideration payable on completion.
  • Approvals required: Namibian Ministry of Industries, Mines & Energy and Upstream Petroleum Unit; Toronto Stock Exchange (Eco’s primary listing).
  • Post-transaction ownership: state-owned National Petroleum Corp of Namibia remains a partner; Eco Atlantic retains 25% interest.
  • Eco’s stated book value for the three licences: USD 11.0m; Eco spent USD 1.4m on the blocks in the year ended 31 March (year not specified in the excerpt).
  • Exploration work outlined: seismic reprocessing of Cooper; 3D surveys of at least 3,000 sq km on Guy and Tamar.
  • Optionality structure (second renewal period in 2028 + drilling commitment): Eco may exercise a put option transferring an additional 10% to BP for a full carry on Eco’s remaining 15%.
  • Carry economics: maximum aggregate carry consideration payable by BP if Eco exercises puts on all three licences is USD 63m, capped at USD 21m per option.
  • Alternative path: Eco can instead retain 25% paying interest and pay 25% of drilling costs, or farm down to other partners.
  • Eco use of proceeds: to support exploration/appraisal across its Atlantic Margin portfolio and for general working capital.
  • Eco CEO quote (Gil Holzman): “This transaction is a clear demonstration of our strategy partnering with supermajors and [international oil companies], to derisk our portfolio while retaining material exposure to significant upside potential with very limited financial requirements from Eco.”
  • BP EVP quote (Gordon Birrell): “Namibia is a region attracting growing industry interest and has a number of exciting frontier basins. This agreement marks BP's entry into the country as an operator, strengthens BP's exploration portfolio and provides long-term growth potential.”

summary: Deutsche Bank downgrades Essentra to hold, citing input cost inflation risks to demand recovery
body:
Time Period Broker rating change (following annual results “last month” per excerpt)

  • Deutsche Bank rating change: lowered Essentra to “hold” from “buy”.
  • Price target cut to 100p from 150p (a 33.3% reduction).
  • DB concern: “meaningful risk” that input cost inflation hits demand, particularly in EMEA, potentially deferring recovery again.
  • DB reasoning reference: post start of Russia/Ukraine war, volume shortfall more than offset inflation-driven pricing benefit.
  • Company reiterated medium-term targets in annual results last month; DB view is it will be difficult without “a sustained period of buoyant demand”.
  • Essentra medium-term targets cited: ~18% adjusted operating margin and ~10% compound annual revenue growth.

summary: Kingfisher launches £75m first tranche of share buyback programme through June 2026
body:
Time Period 10 April 2026 to no later than 30 June 2026 (first tranche of wider buyback programme)

  • Launched a first tranche share repurchase programme (via a non-discretionary programme) to buy shares for cancellation, running from 10 April 2026 to no later than 30 June 2026.
  • Appointed BNP Paribas SA to execute the tranche; BNPP will make independent trading decisions on purchase timing and act as riskless principal for on-selling shares to Kingfisher.
  • Maximum capital allocated to this tranche: £75m (excluding expenses), representing 25% of the £300m total programme announced 24 March 2026.
  • Company will cancel all shares purchased under the tranche (explicitly to reduce share capital).
  • Maximum number of shares potentially purchasable under current authority: 97,886,483 ordinary shares (15 5/7p), factoring in 80,430,840 shares already cancelled since the 23 June 2025 AGM authority.
  • Confirmed no repurchases will be made in respect of American Depositary Receipts (ADRs).
  • Confirmed it currently has no unpublished price sensitive information.

summary: UNITE Group appoints Goldman Sachs as financial adviser and joint corporate broker
body:
Time Period Effective 10 April 2026 (broker/adviser appointment)

  • Appointed Goldman Sachs International as financial adviser and joint corporate broker with immediate effect.
  • Goldman Sachs to work alongside existing corporate brokers J.P. Morgan Cazenove and Deutsche Numis.
  • Goldman Sachs also advising the Board on options for further asset disposals, aligned with Unite’s stated strategy to accelerate repositioning toward a more focused, higher-quality portfolio aligned to the strongest universities.

summary: Land Securities Group will announce FY2026 results on 14 May 2026
body:
Time Period FY ended 31 March 2026 (results date announcement)

  • Will announce full year results for the year ended 31 March 2026 on Thursday 14 May 2026.
  • Results presentation scheduled for 10:00am BST; conference call details to follow ahead of the event.

summary: Kenmare reports strong 2025 sustainability performance including safety improvements and community projects
body:
Time Period Year ended 31 December 2025

  • Published and filed Annual Report for FY ended 31 December 2025 (ESEF-compliant and PDF versions).
  • Managing Director Tom Hickey statement:“2025 represents the second year that Kenmare is reporting in accordance with the Irish transposition of the European Commission’s Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). Although proposed changes to CSRD suggest that we may not be required to do so from year-end 2026, Kenmare will always be committed to delivering best-in-class sustainability performance, regardless of how we are obliged to report on it.

While we announced our operational and financial highlights for the year in our Preliminary Results in March, some of our sustainability highlights include delivering a 30% improvement on our three-year rolling average Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate and planting over 200,000 tree saplings as part of our progressive land rehabilitation programme. The Kenmare Moma Development Association also progressed construction of a district hospital to serve the communities living close to Moma, with phase one over 80% complete by year-end, and built three new water supply systems for local villages. Driven by our purpose of, ‘Transforming resources into opportunity for all’, we remain committed to generating value for all our stakeholders over the long-term.” :contentReference[oaicite:13]{index=13}

  • Flagged possible CSRD rule changes that may mean Kenmare is not required to report under CSRD from year-end 2026 (but intends to maintain best-in-class sustainability regardless).
  • Paper copies of the annual report expected to be posted 13 April 2026 to shareholders who opted for them.
  • Will publish a Sustainability Factbook (Excel) by end of April 2026 to aid investor comparability.
  • Safety metrics: 3-year rolling average LTIFR improved 30% to 0.07 (from 0.1); All Injury Frequency Rate improved to 0.75 (2024: 0.93; ~19% lower YoY).
  • Community investment: $4.0m invested via KMDA (2024: $3.0m; +33% YoY); district hospital phase one >80% complete at year-end; three new village water supply systems.
  • Workforce: 1,684 employees at Moma at year-end; 97% Mozambican representation (unchanged).
  • Environment/resource metrics: >90% electricity from renewable hydropower; >200,000 tree saplings planted; 90% water re-use maintained; >60% waste recycled.
  • Payments to Mozambique government: $34.1m (2023: $34.8m; ~2% lower).
  • Recognition/indices: named Most Transparent Extractive Company in Mozambique for the 5th consecutive year; entered FTSE4Good in June 2025.
  • FY25 mineral product revenue $312.1m, down 20% YoY, driven by 13% lower shipments and 6% lower average price received to $338/t (excl. ZrTi).
  • Recorded an impairment charge of $301.3m (incl. $100.3m recognised in H1 2025), citing lower future revenue projections from uncertain pricing outlook and updated assumptions re potential renewal terms for Moma’s Implementation Agreement (IA).
  • Final dividend suspended due to elevated net debt and weak market conditions; Board intends to resume when prudent and financing permits.
  • Adjusted EBITDA (ex-impairment) $58.0m, down 63% YoY; adjusted EBITDA margin 19%.
  • Adjusted loss after tax $23.7m vs $64.9m profit in 2024 (swing to loss).
  • Total cash operating costs $242.7m, down 0.4% YoY (10% lower finished product production); but cash operating cost per tonne $242, up 11% YoY due to lower volumes.
  • Net debt $158.8m at 31 Dec 2025 vs $25.0m at FY24 (+535%), primarily due to peak capex (~$156m) on WCP A upgrade.
  • In discussions with lenders about RCF adjustments, incl. covenant amendments, given weak market conditions.
  • 2026 capex expected to be significantly reduced: ~$30m remaining development capex on WCP A upgrade + ~$30m sustaining capex; discretionary sustaining items deferred where safe.
  • FY25 production/shipments: HMC production 1,233,300t (-15% YoY); ilmenite production 842,300t (-17% YoY); finished product shipments 947,900t (-13% YoY).
  • Two shipments were partially loaded at year-end (to be reflected in H1 2026 shipping volumes).
  • WCP A upgrade: all major construction/installation work completed.
  • 2026 outlook: expects total shipments >1.1m tonnes with planned stockpile drawdown; expects ilmenite production >800,000 tonnes.
  • IA extension: continued engagement with Mozambique government; referenced two Presidential meetings (June & Nov 2025) and ongoing work toward near-term resolution.

summary: AO World expects 11% revenue growth and £45–£50m adjusted PBT in FY26
body:
Time Period 12 months to 31 March 2026 (FY26 post-close trading update)

  • Expects total Group revenue growth ~11% in FY26.
  • Expects B2C growth ~9.5%, citing market share gains across key categories.
  • Expects adjusted PBT at the top end of upgraded guidance: £45–£50m; implies ~15% YoY adjusted PBT growth (management commentary) despite “material cost headwinds”.
  • Hedging: had arrangements in place ahead of “recent geopolitical developments”, covering ~80% of forecast fuel usage and 100% of electricity usage for the full FY27 trading period.
  • Expects ~£200m liquidity at period end.
  • Free cash flow expected ~£65m (FY25: £23m; +183% YoY) — “profit is converting to cash”.
  • CEO John Roberts quote:“The numbers speak for themselves again and I am delighted to keep doing our talking on the pitch.

“Our shared economics strategy and membership model, built on the foundations of brilliant retail basics, continues to deliver results. We continue to build momentum and all key metrics continue to improve, with an exciting pipeline of new initiatives ahead.

“In the coming weeks, AO will become the first company globally to reach one million Trustpilot reviews with a 4.9 rating from customers. Having founded AO 26 years ago in a small office with three people and a dream, I am incredibly proud to have reached this milestone, which is a testament to the entire team. To achieve that in a logistically difficult category highlights the structural advantage we have been building with world class quality at scale. This underpins our reputation as the UK's most trusted electrical retailer and the value of it sits on our balance sheet at zero.

“I'd like to thank every AOer and every trading partner that has made it possible by embracing our relentless obsession with customers. In line with our values, my gran is delighted and I hope my mum is proud!” :contentReference[oaicite:43]{index=43}

  • Adjusted PBT definition note: FY25 had adjusting items (Music Magpie acquisition expenses £3.3m; post-pay mobile impairment £19.5m); no adjusting items in FY26.
  • Full Year Results (to 31 March 2026) scheduled for 17 June 2026.

summary: UNITE Group reports 74% bed reservations for 2026/27, reiterating occupancy and rental growth guidance
body:
Time Period Trading update and fund valuations as at 31 March 2026 (plus 2026/27 sales cycle progress)

  • 74% of beds reserved for the 2026/27 academic year (2025/26: 76%; -2.6% YoY / -2pp).
  • Reservations mix: 54% of beds reserved via university nomination agreements (2025/26: 58%; -6.9% YoY / -4pp).
  • Reservations mix: 20% of beds reserved via direct-let sales (2025/26: 18%; +11.1% YoY / +2pp).
  • Guidance reiterated (lower end): 93–96% occupancy and 2–3% rental growth for 2026/27 academic year (2025/26 actual: 95.2% occupancy; 4.0% rental growth).
  • CEO Joe Lister quote:“Our strategy is focused on increasing our alignment to the UK's leading universities where we see the strongest prospects for housing demand and future rental growth. To achieve this, we have already increased our disposal programme and the Board is exploring options to further accelerate our transition to a more focused, higher-quality portfolio, which would release surplus capital for reinvestment into share buybacks or University Partnerships consistent with our capital allocation framework.

We are being proactive in driving income through both our partnerships with universities and direct-let sales channels and reservations for the 2026/27 academic year have progressed in line with our expectations as set out at our Preliminary results.” :contentReference[oaicite:50]{index=50}

  • Commentary on demand/partners: active discussions for new multi-year nomination agreements with “high-quality” university partners; some low/mid-tariff universities managing financial exposure until clarity on student numbers; direct-let slightly ahead YoY via pricing/promotions.
  • Hello Student (Empiric) bookings: 33% of rooms reserved for 2026/27 (2025/26: 48%; -31.3% YoY / -15pp), citing delayed sales cycle post tech upgrade (late 2025) + expiry of single-year nomination agreements.
  • Hello Student occupancy expectation reiterated: ~85% for 2026/27, assuming later-cycle acceleration for international/postgraduate demand.
  • Empiric integration: delivered £3m annualised cost synergies to date; targets reiterated for £9m in 2026 and £17m p.a. from 2027 onwards.
  • Utilities hedging: costs fully hedged through 2026 and ~70% for 2027 (forward hedges + multi-year renewable PPAs); commodity element of power/gas ~10% of cost base; management expects recent market events (incl. conflict in Iran cited) not to materially impact 2026 performance.
  • Debt: 100% of group debt fixed or capped at 31 March 2026.
  • Renters’ Rights Act: effective 1 May 2026; existing 2025/26 tenancies subject to transitional arrangements May–September 2026; new PBSA tenancies for 2026/27 exempt; expects changes to be structurally beneficial to PBSA over time (pressure on HMO supply).
  • Disposals: reiterated guidance for £300–£400m (Unite share) disposals in 2026; £130m completed/under offer; additionally marketing/preparing ~£500m of assets for sale over the next 6–12 months (Unite share basis).
  • Specific disposal noted: sale of St Pancras Way to USAF for £126m (gross consideration £186m) expected to complete May.
  • Disposal pipeline includes ~7,000-bed portfolio in “exit cities”/lower-growth locations; described as affordable rents and capital values below replacement cost; lower occupancy/nomination agreements/margins vs wider portfolio (rationale: improve retained estate income visibility/growth).
  • Buyback: acquired 17m shares for £85m out of £100m programme (85% deployed); still targeting completion by 30 June 2026; expects more buybacks once disposals complete; seeking AGM authority to repurchase up to 14.99% of share capital.
  • Development: 719-bed Hawthorne House (Stratford, London) nearing completion; on track to complete June; requires BSR transitional approval before September occupation; ~50% of beds backed by long-term nomination agreement with University of the Arts London.
  • Q1 valuations (like-for-like capital growth): USAF valued £2,798m, -1.7% in quarter (rental growth +0.1%, yield expansion +9bps); weighted average yield 5.4%.
  • Q1 valuations (like-for-like capital growth): LSAV valued £2,034m, -2.4% in quarter (rental growth +0.4%, yield expansion +13bps); weighted average yield 4.8%.
  • Next update: company said it will provide a further trading update on the morning of its AGM on 15 May 2026.

summary: Vertu Motors EBT purchased 40,000 shares at 61.33p for option exercises
body:
Time Period Share purchase notified 9 April 2026 (dealing date 9 April 2026)

  • Employee Benefit Trust (trustee Ocorian Limited) purchased 40,000 ordinary shares (10p) at 61.33p per share on 9 April 2026 (announced 10 April).
  • Shares to be held for EBT purposes and used to satisfy option exercises (including the LTIP for executive directors/other PDMRs).
  • EBT beneficial holding increased from 4,869,788 shares (1.56%) to 4,909,788 shares (1.57%) — +40,000 shares.
  • Issued share capital (voting rights): 312,310,046 ordinary shares; no treasury shares held.

  • Community investment: $4.0m invested via KMDA (2024: $3.0m; +33% YoY); district hospital phase one >80% complete at year-end; three new village water supply systems.
  • Workforce: 1,684 employees at Moma at year-end; 97% Mozambican representation (unchanged).
  • Environment/resource metrics: >90% electricity from renewable hydropower; >200,000 tree saplings planted; 90% water re-use maintained; >60% waste recycled.
  • Payments to Mozambique government: $34.1m (2023: $34.8m; ~2% lower).
  • Recognition/indices: named Most Transparent Extractive Company in Mozambique for the 5th consecutive year; entered FTSE4Good in June 2025.
  • FY25 mineral product revenue $312.1m, down 20% YoY, driven by 13% lower shipments and 6% lower average price received to $338/t (excl. ZrTi).
  • Recorded an impairment charge of $301.3m (incl. $100.3m recognised in H1 2025), citing lower future revenue projections from uncertain pricing outlook and updated assumptions re potential renewal terms for Moma’s Implementation Agreement (IA).
  • Final dividend suspended due to elevated net debt and weak market conditions; Board intends to resume when prudent and financing permits.
  • Adjusted EBITDA (ex-impairment) $58.0m, down 63% YoY; adjusted EBITDA margin 19%.
  • Adjusted loss after tax $23.7m vs $64.9m profit in 2024 (swing to loss).
  • Total cash operating costs $242.7m, down 0.4% YoY (10% lower finished product production); but cash operating cost per tonne $242, up 11% YoY due to lower volumes.
  • Net debt $158.8m at 31 Dec 2025 vs $25.0m at FY24 (+535%), primarily due to peak capex (~$156m) on WCP A upgrade.
  • In discussions with lenders about RCF adjustments, incl. covenant amendments, given weak market conditions.
  • 2026 capex expected to be significantly reduced: ~$30m remaining development capex on WCP A upgrade + ~$30m sustaining capex; discretionary sustaining items deferred where safe.
  • FY25 production/shipments: HMC production 1,233,300t (-15% YoY); ilmenite production 842,300t (-17% YoY); finished product shipments 947,900t (-13% YoY).
  • Two shipments were partially loaded at year-end (to be reflected in H1 2026 shipping volumes).
  • WCP A upgrade: all major construction/installation work completed.
  • 2026 outlook: expects total shipments >1.1m tonnes with planned stockpile drawdown; expects ilmenite production >800,000 tonnes.
  • IA extension: continued engagement with Mozambique government; referenced two Presidential meetings (June & Nov 2025) and ongoing work toward near-term resolution.

summary: AO World expects 11% revenue growth and £45-50m adjusted PBT in FY26
body:
Time Period 12 months to 31 March 2026 (FY26 post-close trading update)

  • Expects total Group revenue growth ~11% in FY26.
  • Expects B2C growth ~9.5%, citing market share gains across key categories.
  • Expects adjusted PBT at the top end of upgraded guidance: £45–£50m; implies ~15% YoY adjusted PBT growth (management commentary) despite “material cost headwinds”.
  • Hedging: had arrangements in place ahead of “recent geopolitical developments”, covering ~80% of forecast fuel usage and 100% of electricity usage for the full FY27 trading period.
  • Expects ~£200m liquidity at period end.
  • Free cash flow expected ~£65m (FY25: £23m; +183% YoY) — “profit is converting to cash”.
  • CEO John Roberts quote:“The numbers speak for themselves again and I am delighted to keep doing our talking on the pitch.

“Our shared economics strategy and membership model, built on the foundations of brilliant retail basics, continues to deliver results. We continue to build momentum and all key metrics continue to improve, with an exciting pipeline of new initiatives ahead.

“In the coming weeks, AO will become the first company globally to reach one million Trustpilot reviews with a 4.9 rating from customers. Having founded AO 26 years ago in a small office with three people and a dream, I am incredibly proud to have reached this milestone, which is a testament to the entire team. To achieve that in a logistically difficult category highlights the structural advantage we have been building with world class quality at scale. This underpins our reputation as the UK's most trusted electrical retailer and the value of it sits on our balance sheet at zero.

“I'd like to thank every AOer and every trading partner that has made it possible by embracing our relentless obsession with customers. In line with our values, my gran is delighted and I hope my mum is proud!” :contentReference[oaicite:43]{index=43}

  • Adjusted PBT definition note: FY25 had adjusting items (Music Magpie acquisition expenses £3.3m; post-pay mobile impairment £19.5m); no adjusting items in FY26.
  • Full Year Results (to 31 March 2026) scheduled for 17 June 2026.

summary: UNITE Group sees slower bed reservations but maintains 2026/27 guidance amid portfolio transition
body:
Time Period Trading update and fund valuations as at 31 March 2026 (plus 2026/27 sales cycle progress)

  • 74% of beds reserved for the 2026/27 academic year (2025/26: 76%; -2.6% YoY / -2pp).
  • Reservations mix: 54% of beds reserved via university nomination agreements (2025/26: 58%; -6.9% YoY / -4pp).
  • Reservations mix: 20% of beds reserved via direct-let sales (2025/26: 18%; +11.1% YoY / +2pp).
  • Guidance reiterated (lower end): 93–96% occupancy and 2–3% rental growth for 2026/27 academic year (2025/26 actual: 95.2% occupancy; 4.0% rental growth).
  • CEO Joe Lister quote:“Our strategy is focused on increasing our alignment to the UK's leading universities where we see the strongest prospects for housing demand and future rental growth. To achieve this, we have already increased our disposal programme and the Board is exploring options to further accelerate our transition to a more focused, higher-quality portfolio, which would release surplus capital for reinvestment into share buybacks or University Partnerships consistent with our capital allocation framework.

We are being proactive in driving income through both our partnerships with universities and direct-let sales channels and reservations for the 2026/27 academic year have progressed in line with our expectations as set out at our Preliminary results.” :contentReference[oaicite:50]{index=50}

  • Commentary on demand/partners: active discussions for new multi-year nomination agreements with “high-quality” university partners; some low/mid-tariff universities managing financial exposure until clarity on student numbers; direct-let slightly ahead YoY via pricing/promotions.
  • Hello Student (Empiric) bookings: 33% of rooms reserved for 2026/27 (2025/26: 48%; -31.3% YoY / -15pp), citing delayed sales cycle post tech upgrade (late 2025) + expiry of single-year nomination agreements.
  • Hello Student occupancy expectation reiterated: ~85% for 2026/27, assuming later-cycle acceleration for international/postgraduate demand.
  • Empiric integration: delivered £3m annualised cost synergies to date; targets reiterated for £9m in 2026 and £17m p.a. from 2027 onwards.
  • Utilities hedging: costs fully hedged through 2026 and ~70% for 2027 (forward hedges + multi-year renewable PPAs); commodity element of power/gas ~10% of cost base; management expects recent market events (incl. conflict in Iran cited) not to materially impact 2026 performance.
  • Debt: 100% of group debt fixed or capped at 31 March 2026.
  • Renters’ Rights Act: effective 1 May 2026; existing 2025/26 tenancies subject to transitional arrangements May–September 2026; new PBSA tenancies for 2026/27 exempt; expects changes to be structurally beneficial to PBSA over time (pressure on HMO supply).
  • Disposals: reiterated guidance for £300–£400m (Unite share) disposals in 2026; £130m completed/under offer; additionally marketing/preparing ~£500m of assets for sale over the next 6–12 months (Unite share basis).
  • Specific disposal noted: sale of St Pancras Way to USAF for £126m (gross consideration £186m) expected to complete May.
  • Disposal pipeline includes ~7,000-bed portfolio in “exit cities”/lower-growth locations; described as affordable rents and capital values below replacement cost; lower occupancy/nomination agreements/margins vs wider portfolio (rationale: improve retained estate income visibility/growth).
  • Buyback: acquired 17m shares for £85m out of £100m programme (85% deployed); still targeting completion by 30 June 2026; expects more buybacks once disposals complete; seeking AGM authority to repurchase up to 14.99% of share capital.
  • Development: 719-bed Hawthorne House (Stratford, London) nearing completion; on track to complete June; requires BSR transitional approval before September occupation; ~50% of beds backed by long-term nomination agreement with University of the Arts London.
  • Q1 valuations (like-for-like capital growth): USAF valued £2,798m, -1.7% in quarter (rental growth +0.1%, yield expansion +9bps); weighted average yield 5.4%.
  • Q1 valuations (like-for-like capital growth): LSAV valued £2,034m, -2.4% in quarter (rental growth +0.4%, yield expansion +13bps); weighted average yield 4.8%.
  • Next update: company said it will provide a further trading update on the morning of its AGM on 15 May 2026.

summary: Amazon launches AI-powered pet adoption matching tool with Best Friends Animal Society
body:
Time Period N/A (product / campaign launch announcement)

  • Amazon launched its first-ever pet adoption experience: an AI-powered matching tool that takes natural-language prompts (e.g., “low-energy dog good for apartments”) and returns adoptable dog/cat matches from Best Friends’ centres nationwide.
  • The tool was developed with PetArmor and Best Friends Animal Society; it searches Best Friends’ database across centres in Houston, Los Angeles, New York City, Northwest Arkansas, Salt Lake City, plus the Best Friends Animal Sanctuary (Kanab, Utah).
  • The campaign (“Protect Playtime”) includes five elements: AI-powered discovery, generative video for adoptable pets, building shelter infrastructure, passive charitable giving via streaming, and shoppable pet products.
  • Best Friends data point cited: “every 90 seconds a dog or cat is killed in a U.S. shelter” due to lacking a safe place to call home.
  • Best Friends data point cited: ~7 million households will add a pet over the next year; if 6% more of them adopt from a shelter, the US could reach “no-kill” (as defined in the release).
  • Quote (Amazon Ads Brand Innovation Lab, Lauren Anderson): “The best part of working on this was aligning everything around one question: ‘how do we help more of our country’s adoptable pets in shelters find the healthy, happy homes they deserve?’ … ‘It’s a true full-funnel campaign on a worthy mission.’”
  • The AI considers matching factors including temperament, energy level, living situation compatibility, and household composition, and returns pet profiles with photos + links to complete adoption applications.
  • Amazon Ads created personalised animated videos for featured pets to help adopters visualise the pet in a home environment (turning static profiles into “emotional narratives”).
  • “Physical infrastructure” component: in February, PetArmor + Amazon built a custom dog park and “catio” at Glen Rose Animal Control (Texas), transforming a Best Friends partner shelter.
  • Impact metric from the Glen Rose event: the shelter’s Valentine’s Day adoption event placed “two dozen” cats and dogs (24), “more than quadrupling” the prior single-day adoption record (>4x / >300% higher).
  • Video documentation of the transformation runs until 31 July across Amazon properties (incl. Prime Video, Streaming TV ads, and PetArmor’s Amazon Brand Store).
  • Quote (Best Friends CEO, Julie Castle): “This innovative campaign will make a meaningful impact… by giving people new ways to connect with adoptable pets…”
  • Additional activation: “Stream It Forward” on Fire TV—Amazon will donate to Best Friends for each hour of curated animal-themed entertainment watched (titles cited include Scooby Doo, Tom & Jerry, Ratatouille, Lilo & Stitch).
  • Quote (PetArmor / PetIQ, Kyle Lembke): “Now we’re protecting their chance at finding a loving home… we’re removing the barriers between pets in shelters and the families who will love them.”

summary: Experian partners with Taylor Rooks for April Financial Literacy Month campaign promoting financial education
body:
Time Period April 2026 (Financial Literacy Month campaign)

  • Experian partnered with journalist/on-air personality Taylor Rooks for a Financial Literacy Month campaign running throughout April.
  • Campaign positioning: “approachable, real-life conversations about credit and money management,” with Rooks sharing her personal financial journey (credit + overall financial health).
  • Experian will donate $50,000 to the Taylor Rooks Foundation (mission described as expanding access to education, health, and opportunity in underserved communities).
  • Quote (Taylor Rooks): “Partnering with Experian felt natural because… financial knowledge is one of the most powerful tools a person can have…”
  • Experian brand framing: reinforces being consumers’ “Big Financial Friend™” and the mission “Financial Power To All™”.
  • Quote (Steve Hartmann, VP Marketing, Experian Consumer Services): “We chose to partner with Taylor because she embodies authenticity and relatability… Her platform in sports and media allows us to connect with broader and more diverse audiences…”
  • Distribution: videos across Experian’s YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, X and TikTok, plus paid activations on TikTok, Snapchat, Meta, YouTube and most streaming platforms.
  • Rooks also launched a 30-day series of daily financial tips as part of the initiative.

summary: Balfour Beatty completes major Charleston multifamily and senior living projects while advancing adaptive reuse developments
body:
Time Period N/A (project delivery / progress update)

  • Update focused on Balfour Beatty’s building projects in the Charleston (US) area, citing momentum across adaptive reuse, mixed-use, hospitality, multifamily, and senior living.
  • Completed: “The Charles” (Charleston waterfront district) — 66,250 sq ft multifamily development with 15 three-story townhomes and 8 residential flats across three buildings.
  • The Charles: developed by The Beach Company (described as a long-standing local client); design details cited include historical cast stone, brick, and decorative wrought iron railings.
  • Completed: Seafield’s at Kiawah Island — described as the first luxury Life Plan Community in the Kiawah/Seabrook Island area; 360,000 sq ft facility including independent living residences plus assisted living units.
  • Seafield’s amenities list includes: concierge services, Technogym-designed fitness centre, resort-style pool, wellness/yoga studio, theatre, library/tech bar, bistro and multiple dining options.
  • Seafield’s differentiator cited: in-house medical clinic operated by MUSC Health (Medical University of South Carolina).
  • In progress: redevelopment of Pacific Box (Upper Peninsula, Charleston) with Asana Partners (repeat client) — 221,600 sq ft adaptive reuse project, scheduled for completion in Q2 2026.
  • Pacific Box upgrades cited: new premium stone cladding + storefront glass, new retail facades to increase natural light, enhanced central courtyard + turf areas for year-round programming.
  • In progress: construction of the Emanuel Nine Memorial (delivered on behalf of repeat client The Beach Company), framed as a community-unifying project.
  • Quote (Jeff Sandeen, President, Balfour Beatty Carolinas): “Charleston is a market where our team’s expertise and commitment truly shine… We look forward to continuing our relentless efforts in delivering exceptional results for our clients and communities here.”

summary: Colin Bell appointed non-executive director of Julius Baer Group effective April 9 2026
body:
Time Period Effective 9 April 2026 (director appointment disclosure)

  • Colin Bell (Man Group non-executive director) appointed as a non-executive director of Julius Baer Group Ltd (SIX Swiss Exchange-listed), effective 9 April 2026.
  • At Julius Baer, Bell will also join the Governance & Risk Committee and the Development & Innovation Committee.

summary: Wetherspoon corrects Sun report claiming CEO opposed QR codes and app technology
body:
Time Period N/A (statement / correction)

  • RNS issued as a “NEWS RELEASE – CORRECTION” responding to a Sun newspaper article dated 7 April (“Wetherspoons boss slams rise of QR codes…”).
  • Wetherspoon says the Sun reported Tim Martin “bemoaned the rise of QR codes” and “may even decide to call time on the use of technology,” but the company states this is not accurate.
  • Wetherspoon states Martin’s actual context: replying to a customer letter in Wetherspoon News criticising the app/QR code, Martin joked that he agreed because app users can’t “[shoot] the breeze with the bar team…impressing them with … witty observations”.
  • The company says: “The Sun story has no basis in fact and has, unfortunately, created anxiety among dedicated app-users.”
  • Wetherspoon states explicitly: “Mr Martin has made it clear that the app is here to stay.”
  • Full quote (Tim Martin): “The journalist undoubtedly had a rush of blood to the head and struggled to understand my deft sense of humour. The app is very popular and Wetherspoon never had any intention of curbing its use.”

Wagler (EVP & CFO), and Barry Schneider (President & COO). Replay window: available on company website until 11:59 p.m. EDT on 28 April 2026; MP3 download also to be provided.

summary: Experian wins top Chartis retail banking analytics awards for its AI-powered platform strategy
body: Time Period Chartis “Retail Banking Analytics50 2025” recognition (report year 2025) Recognised as a top vendor in Chartis Research’s inaugural Retail Banking Analytics50 2025. Won “Best Overall Strategy” award. Won “Retail Analytics Governance Framework” award. Chartis Research quote (Anish Shah): “Experian continues to set a benchmark in retail banking analytics. Its cloud-native, AI-powered platform delivers fast, transparent and accurate risk decisioning – as reflected in its top 3 placing in Chartis’ Retail Banking Analytics50 2025 ranking.” Experian positions Experian Ascend Platform™ as an integrated stack for data exploration, model deployment/monitoring, and model governance (framed as enabling more robust/compliant analytics). Experian quote (Vijay Mehta): “For more than 15 years, our AI-powered solutions have enabled financial institutions around the world to transform data into actionable insights, applying advanced analytics and modeling to drive smarter decisions and bring consumer-centric offerings to market. This recognition underscores our continued innovation in helping customers streamline decision-making, strengthen fraud prevention, and maintain compliance with evolving data privacy regulations.”

summary: Intel and Google expand AI partnership to co-develop custom infrastructure processors
body:
Time Period Multiyear partnership announcement (no financial period)

  • Intel and Google announced a multiyear collaboration to advance AI / cloud infrastructure, explicitly reinforcing the role of CPUs + custom IPUs in “heterogeneous” AI systems.
  • Stated deployment: Google Cloud continues using Intel Xeon across workload-optimised instances; cites “latest Xeon 6” powering C4 and N4 instances (AI coordination, inference, general-purpose workloads).
  • Expansion area: co-development of custom ASIC-based infrastructure processing units (IPUs) to offload networking/storage/security from host CPUs to improve utilisation, efficiency and predictability at hyperscale.
  • Intel CEO quote: “AI is reshaping how infrastructure is built and scaled… Scaling AI requires more than accelerators - it requires balanced systems. CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand.”
  • Google (Amin Vahdat) quote: “CPUs and infrastructure acceleration remain a cornerstone of AI systems—from training orchestration to inference and deployment… Intel has been a trusted partner for nearly two decades, and their Xeon roadmap gives us confidence that we can continue to meet the growing performance and efficiency demands of our workloads.”

summary: PayPal Payment Links integrated into Canva enabling 265 million users to monetize designs
body:
Time Period Product / partnership launch (no financial period)

  • PayPal Payment Links now available directly in Canva, positioned as enabling Canva’s “265 million monthly users worldwide” to convert designs into checkout experiences.
  • Value prop: creators can embed a PayPal-hosted checkout via payment link or QR code into digital/printed designs and accept payments “with no website required.”
  • Payment methods called out: PayPal checkout including PayPal, Venmo, and PayPal Pay Later (with the note: Venmo payments only available in the US; Pay Later in eligible markets).
  • Monetisation framing: points to social commerce (claims projected to surpass $1tn by 2028) and argues transactions are shifting “inside content, conversations, and communities.”
  • PayPal quote (Taira Hall): “By pairing PayPal's trusted global payment infrastructure with Canva's creative workflow, we're reducing the friction between inspiration and income and meeting them at point of need. With PayPal integrated directly in Canva, creators can move seamlessly from creating to getting paid.”
  • Canva quote (Emily MacDonald): “Whether someone's launching their first product, booking their next clients, or selling at a weekend market, having PayPal Payment Links right inside Canva means you can go from a bold idea to getting paid in just a few clicks, without ever leaving their design.”
  • Marketing/event: PayPal named “Official Payment Partner” of Canva Create on 16 April 2026 (Hollywood Park, Los Angeles); app available globally via Canva Marketplace.

Playtech PLC (and Evolution AB litigation update) Time Period US lawsuit / proposed defendant expansion (current)

  • Evolution says it has asked the Superior Court of New Jersey for permission to add Playtech as a defendant in its defamation suit (also against Calcagni & Kanefsky LLP and Black Cube).
  • Evolution allegation: Playtech hired Black Cube “to create and publicise a defamatory report designed to harm Evolution,” and that this amounted to a “smear campaign… to eliminate competition in the North American marketplace.”
  • Evolution says the filing accuses Playtech of “trade libel, fraud and racketeering” and alleges withholding information from shareholders; also alleges Playtech CEO Mor Weizer’s “active participation.”
  • Evolution claim on incentives: alleges Playtech promised Black Cube £1.5m in success fees.
  • Evolution claim re regulators: “Playtech and Black Cube's agreement outlined plans... to get regulators to open an investigation and ultimately revoke Evolution's gaming license.” It adds that the report “was determined by two state gaming regulators in the US to lack evidentiary support.”
  • Playtech response: called the claims “baseless and without merit,” adding: “Playtech stands by both the decision to commission the report at the centre of Evolution's claims, and the validity of its findings.”
  • Playtech counter-attack (quote): “Evolution continues to seek to avoid legitimate scrutiny rather than addressing longstanding questions about its own conduct, including its decision to supply operators in both illegal and sanctioned markets and to support unlicensed operators in regulated markets.”
  • Evolution rebuttal: stated Playtech “currently operates or has operated in some of the very markets it accuses Evolution of operating in illegally,” and reaffirmed confidence in its compliance policies.

summary: Playtech denies Evolution's legal claims as baseless and welcomes court proceedings
body:
Time Period Update on Evolution AB litigation step (referencing Evolution public statement on 21 October 2025)

  • Evolution AB stated it has requested permission from the Superior Court of New Jersey to add Playtech to Evolution’s ongoing legal case; Playtech says it had been “awaiting this development” since Evolution’s 21 October 2025 statement.
  • Playtech position: Evolution’s claims are “baseless and without merit.”
  • Playtech reiterates it stands by (i) the decision to commission the report at the centre of Evolution’s claims and (ii) the validity of the report’s findings.
  • Playtech escalates allegations toward Evolution: says Evolution is avoiding scrutiny and references questions about Evolution’s conduct, including supplying operators in illegal and sanctioned markets and supporting unlicensed operators in regulated markets.
  • Playtech welcomes court and regulatory examination and specifically highlights wanting to participate in discovery (including questioning Evolution employees/executives/officers in court).
  • Playtech: “The Company is very confident” (citing its evidence, including “recent additional evidence”) that proceedings will confirm the report’s credibility/legitimacy and the importance of the issues raised.
  • Playtech says it will defend itself vigorously and continue acting in the best interests of operators, suppliers, regulators, and shareholders.

summary: Shaftesbury Capital declares 2.1p final dividend payable May 2026 as property income distribution
body:
Time Period 2025 final cash dividend (payment timetable May 2026)

  • Confirms additional info for 2025 final cash dividend of 2.1p per share, to be paid wholly as a property income distribution (PID).
  • Dividend payment date: Friday, 22 May 2026; record date: Friday, 24 April 2026.
  • Dividend remains subject to shareholder approval at AGM on Thursday, 14 May 2026.
  • Declared ZAR exchange rate for the dividend: 22.0544 ZAR : 1 GBP, determined Wednesday, 8 April 2026.
  • Based on this, South African register holders receive 46.31424 ZAR cents gross per share; 37.05139 ZAR cents net of UK withholding tax.
  • UK withholding tax stated at 20% (unless exemptions apply): 0.42p (and 9.26285 ZAR cents).
  • PID net of UK withholding tax: 1.68p (and 37.05139 ZAR cents).
  • States there will be no Non-PID element of the proposed final dividend.
  • South African dividends tax discussion: mentions potential effective 5% SA Dividends Tax (where applicable) and a separate scenario where 20% SA Dividends Tax applies, with example net amounts 34.73568 ZAR cents (effective 5%) vs 27.78854 ZAR cents (20% SA rate), after UK withholding and SA Dividends Tax.
  • Notes SA shareholders may apply to HMRC after payment for a refund of the difference between 20% UK withholding and the UK/SA treaty rate of 15% (as described).

summary: Gina Jardine appointed Non-Executive Director of Resolute Mining effective May 2026
body:
Time Period Appointment to take effect following Resolute Mining AGM on 20 May 2026

  • Gina Jardine (Forterra Non-Executive Director) will be appointed Non-Executive Director of Resolute Mining Limited (listed on LSE main market and ASX), following the AGM on 20 May 2026.

summary: Great Portland Estates fully lets SIX St Andrew Street to AI company, achieving strong rental returns
body:
Time Period Letting / leasing update as at 9 April 2026 (includes FY-end leasing momentum commentary)

  • Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street, EC4, completing leasing at the Fully Managed development (building now fully occupied).
  • The 3rd and 4th floors (total 11,680 sq ft) were let to an AI company.
  • Asset economics: now generating £8.8m annual rent at an average £200/sq ft.
  • Rent achieved is 6.2% ahead of ERV.
  • Reported 6.2% yield on cost for the building.
  • Portfolio mix shift: AI-related customers now represent 11.7% of GPE’s total office portfolio and 25.2% of its Fully Managed offices.
  • Fully Managed leasing momentum: activity “accelerated” in the final quarter with 19 deals completed and 7 further deals under offer, supported by “record” viewings and enquiries.
  • Building spec: 8 floors of refurbished Fully Managed offices (units 1,200–5,800 sq ft) with shared amenities (communal lounge/boardroom, rooftop terrace, wellness studio).
  • Quote (Molly Maguire, Senior Flex Leasing Manager): “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.”

summary: Me Group partners with ASDA to deploy up to 700 laundry machines across UK sites
body:
Time Period Partnership announcement; roll-out ambition and FY 2026 targets

  • Signed a new partnership with ASDA to install laundry machines across ASDA sites in the UK.
  • Scope: ME Group will install and operate machines at ASDA Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites.
  • Roll-out ambition: target up to 700 machines across ASDA sites (described as the “overall ambition”).
  • Current footprint: 7,600+ machines across 12 countries; 1,500+ located in the UK & Republic of Ireland.
  • Product spec highlights: self-service, large-capacity 9kg and 20kg drums; energy-efficient, high-speed wash/dry; available 24/7.
  • FY 2026 ambition: install more than 1,300 machines.
  • Long-term ambition: 20,000+ machines globally.
  • Stated 2025 delivery: “record” installations of 1,326 across geographies.
  • Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “ASDA is a perfect company to partner with… this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.”
  • Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “The rapid expansion of our laundry services is a key strategic priority… This partnership is indicative of our intention to further cement our UK market-leading position in the unattended laundry segment.”
  • Quote (Joseph Sutton, VP Asda Express, foodservice and fuel): “This large-scale rollout of units gives customers a quick, convenient and great-value way to tackle their laundry while they shop…”

summary: Amazon launches AI-powered pet adoption matching tool with Best Friends Animal Society
body:
Time Period N/A (product / campaign launch announcement)

  • Amazon launched its first-ever pet adoption experience: an AI-powered matching tool that takes natural-language prompts (e.g., “low-energy dog good for apartments”) and returns adoptable dog/cat matches from Best Friends’ centres nationwide.
  • The tool was developed with PetArmor and Best Friends Animal Society; it searches Best Friends’ database across centres in Houston, Los Angeles, New York City, Northwest Arkansas, Salt Lake City, plus the Best Friends Animal Sanctuary (Kanab, Utah).
  • The campaign (“Protect Playtime”) includes five elements: AI-powered discovery, generative video for adoptable pets, building shelter infrastructure, passive charitable giving via streaming, and shoppable pet products.
  • Best Friends data point cited: “every 90 seconds a dog or cat is killed in a U.S. shelter” due to lacking a safe place to call home.
  • Best Friends data point cited: ~7 million households will add a pet over the next year; if 6% more of them adopt from a shelter, the US could reach “no-kill” (as defined in the release).
  • Quote (Amazon Ads Brand Innovation Lab, Lauren Anderson): “The best part of working on this was aligning everything around one question: ‘how do we help more of our country’s adoptable pets in shelters find the healthy, happy homes they deserve?’ … ‘It’s a true full-funnel campaign on a worthy mission.’”
  • The AI considers matching factors including temperament, energy level, living situation compatibility, and household composition, and returns pet profiles with photos + links to complete adoption applications.
  • Amazon Ads created personalised animated videos for featured pets to help adopters visualise the pet in a home environment (turning static profiles into “emotional narratives”).
  • “Physical infrastructure” component: in February, PetArmor + Amazon built a custom dog park and “catio” at Glen Rose Animal Control (Texas), transforming a Best Friends partner shelter.
  • Impact metric from the Glen Rose event: the shelter’s Valentine’s Day adoption event placed “two dozen” cats and dogs (24), “more than quadrupling” the prior single-day adoption record (>4x / >300% higher).
  • Video documentation of the transformation runs until 31 July across Amazon properties (incl. Prime Video, Streaming TV ads, and PetArmor’s Amazon Brand Store).
  • Quote (Best Friends CEO, Julie Castle): “This innovative campaign will make a meaningful impact… by giving people new ways to connect with adoptable pets…”
  • Additional activation: “Stream It Forward” on Fire TV—Amazon will donate to Best Friends for each hour of curated animal-themed entertainment watched (titles cited include Scooby Doo, Tom & Jerry, Ratatouille, Lilo & Stitch).
  • Quote (PetArmor / PetIQ, Kyle Lembke): “Now we’re protecting their chance at finding a loving home… we’re removing the barriers between pets in shelters and the families who will love them.”

summary: Experian partners with Taylor Rooks for Financial Literacy Month campaign donating $50,000
body:
Time Period April 2026 (Financial Literacy Month campaign)

  • Experian partnered with journalist/on-air personality Taylor Rooks for a Financial Literacy Month campaign running throughout April.
  • Campaign positioning: “approachable, real-life conversations about credit and money management,” with Rooks sharing her personal financial journey (credit + overall financial health).
  • Experian will donate $50,000 to the Taylor Rooks Foundation (mission described as expanding access to education, health, and opportunity in underserved communities).
  • Quote (Taylor Rooks): “Partnering with Experian felt natural because… financial knowledge is one of the most powerful tools a person can have…”
  • Experian brand framing: reinforces being consumers’ “Big Financial Friend™” and the mission “Financial Power To All™”.
  • Quote (Steve Hartmann, VP Marketing, Experian Consumer Services): “We chose to partner with Taylor because she embodies authenticity and relatability… Her platform in sports and media allows us to connect with broader and more diverse audiences…”
  • Distribution: videos across Experian’s YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, X and TikTok, plus paid activations on TikTok, Snapchat, Meta, YouTube and most streaming platforms.
  • Rooks also launched a 30-day series of daily financial tips as part of the initiative.

summary: Balfour Beatty completes Charleston multifamily and senior living projects with adaptive reuse momentum
body:
Time Period N/A (project delivery / progress update)

  • Update focused on Balfour Beatty’s building projects in the Charleston (US) area, citing momentum across adaptive reuse, mixed-use, hospitality, multifamily, and senior living.
  • Completed: “The Charles” (Charleston waterfront district) — 66,250 sq ft multifamily development with 15 three-story townhomes and 8 residential flats across three buildings.
  • The Charles: developed by The Beach Company (described as a long-standing local client); design details cited include historical cast stone, brick, and decorative wrought iron railings.
  • Completed: Seafield’s at Kiawah Island — described as the first luxury Life Plan Community in the Kiawah/Seabrook Island area; 360,000 sq ft facility including independent living residences plus assisted living units.
  • Seafield’s amenities list includes: concierge services, Technogym-designed fitness centre, resort-style pool, wellness/yoga studio, theatre, library/tech bar, bistro and multiple dining options.
  • Seafield’s differentiator cited: in-house medical clinic operated by MUSC Health (Medical University of South Carolina).
  • In progress: redevelopment of Pacific Box (Upper Peninsula, Charleston) with Asana Partners (repeat client) — 221,600 sq ft adaptive reuse project, scheduled for completion in Q2 2026.
  • Pacific Box upgrades cited: new premium stone cladding + storefront glass, new retail facades to increase natural light, enhanced central courtyard + turf areas for year-round programming.
  • In progress: construction of the Emanuel Nine Memorial (delivered on behalf of repeat client The Beach Company), framed as a community-unifying project.
  • Quote (Jeff Sandeen, President, Balfour Beatty Carolinas): “Charleston is a market where our team’s expertise and commitment truly shine… We look forward to continuing our relentless efforts in delivering exceptional results for our clients and communities here.”

summary: Wetherspoon clarifies Tim Martin's comments were misreported; app will continue
body:
Time Period N/A (statement / correction)

  • RNS issued as a “NEWS RELEASE – CORRECTION” responding to a Sun newspaper article dated 7 April (“Wetherspoons boss slams rise of QR codes…”).
  • Wetherspoon says the Sun reported Tim Martin “bemoaned the rise of QR codes” and “may even decide to call time on the use of technology,” but the company states this is not accurate.
  • Wetherspoon states Martin’s actual context: replying to a customer letter in Wetherspoon News criticising the app/QR code, Martin joked that he agreed because app users can’t “[shoot] the breeze with the bar team…impressing them with … witty observations”.
  • The company says: “The Sun story has no basis in fact and has, unfortunately, created anxiety among dedicated app-users.”
  • Wetherspoon states explicitly: “Mr Martin has made it clear that the app is here to stay.”
  • Full quote (Tim Martin): “The journalist undoubtedly had a rush of blood to the head and struggled to understand my deft sense of humour. The app is very popular and Wetherspoon never had any intention of curbing its use.”
