summary: Experian wins top Chartis retail banking analytics award for AI-powered platform strategy
body: Time Period Chartis “Retail Banking Analytics50 2025” recognition (report year 2025) Recognised as a top vendor in Chartis Research’s inaugural Retail Banking Analytics50 2025. Won “Best Overall Strategy” award. Won “Retail Analytics Governance Framework” award. Chartis Research quote (Anish Shah): “Experian continues to set a benchmark in retail banking analytics. Its cloud-native, AI-powered platform delivers fast, transparent and accurate risk decisioning – as reflected in its top 3 placing in Chartis’ Retail Banking Analytics50 2025 ranking.” Experian positions Experian Ascend Platform™ as an integrated stack for data exploration, model deployment/monitoring, and model governance (framed as enabling more robust/compliant analytics). Experian quote (Vijay Mehta): “For more than 15 years, our AI-powered solutions have enabled financial institutions around the world to transform data into actionable insights, applying advanced analytics and modeling to drive smarter decisions and bring consumer-centric offerings to market. This recognition underscores our continued innovation in helping customers streamline decision-making, strengthen fraud prevention, and maintain compliance with evolving data privacy regulations.”

summary: Intel and Google expand partnership to develop custom AI infrastructure processors alongside Xeon CPUs
body:
Time Period Multiyear partnership announcement (no financial period)

  • Intel and Google announced a multiyear collaboration to advance AI / cloud infrastructure, explicitly reinforcing the role of CPUs + custom IPUs in “heterogeneous” AI systems.
  • Stated deployment: Google Cloud continues using Intel Xeon across workload-optimised instances; cites “latest Xeon 6” powering C4 and N4 instances (AI coordination, inference, general-purpose workloads).
  • Expansion area: co-development of custom ASIC-based infrastructure processing units (IPUs) to offload networking/storage/security from host CPUs to improve utilisation, efficiency and predictability at hyperscale.
  • Intel CEO quote: “AI is reshaping how infrastructure is built and scaled… Scaling AI requires more than accelerators - it requires balanced systems. CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand.”
  • Google (Amin Vahdat) quote: “CPUs and infrastructure acceleration remain a cornerstone of AI systems—from training orchestration to inference and deployment… Intel has been a trusted partner for nearly two decades, and their Xeon roadmap gives us confidence that we can continue to meet the growing performance and efficiency demands of our workloads.”

summary: PayPal Payment Links now available directly in Canva for creators to accept payments
body:
Time Period Product / partnership launch (no financial period)

  • PayPal Payment Links now available directly in Canva, positioned as enabling Canva’s “265 million monthly users worldwide” to convert designs into checkout experiences.
  • Value prop: creators can embed a PayPal-hosted checkout via payment link or QR code into digital/printed designs and accept payments “with no website required.”
  • Payment methods called out: PayPal checkout including PayPal, Venmo, and PayPal Pay Later (with the note: Venmo payments only available in the US; Pay Later in eligible markets).
  • Monetisation framing: points to social commerce (claims projected to surpass $1tn by 2028) and argues transactions are shifting “inside content, conversations, and communities.”
  • PayPal quote (Taira Hall): “By pairing PayPal's trusted global payment infrastructure with Canva's creative workflow, we're reducing the friction between inspiration and income and meeting them at point of need. With PayPal integrated directly in Canva, creators can move seamlessly from creating to getting paid.”
  • Canva quote (Emily MacDonald): “Whether someone's launching their first product, booking their next clients, or selling at a weekend market, having PayPal Payment Links right inside Canva means you can go from a bold idea to getting paid in just a few clicks, without ever leaving their design.”
  • Marketing/event: PayPal named “Official Payment Partner” of Canva Create on 16 April 2026 (Hollywood Park, Los Angeles); app available globally via Canva Marketplace.

Playtech PLC (and Evolution AB litigation update) Time Period US lawsuit / proposed defendant expansion (current)

  • Evolution says it has asked the Superior Court of New Jersey for permission to add Playtech as a defendant in its defamation suit (also against Calcagni & Kanefsky LLP and Black Cube).
  • Evolution allegation: Playtech hired Black Cube “to create and publicise a defamatory report designed to harm Evolution,” and that this amounted to a “smear campaign… to eliminate competition in the North American marketplace.”
  • Evolution says the filing accuses Playtech of “trade libel, fraud and racketeering” and alleges withholding information from shareholders; also alleges Playtech CEO Mor Weizer’s “active participation.”
  • Evolution claim on incentives: alleges Playtech promised Black Cube £1.5m in success fees.
  • Evolution claim re regulators: “Playtech and Black Cube's agreement outlined plans... to get regulators to open an investigation and ultimately revoke Evolution's gaming license.” It adds that the report “was determined by two state gaming regulators in the US to lack evidentiary support.”
  • Playtech response: called the claims “baseless and without merit,” adding: “Playtech stands by both the decision to commission the report at the centre of Evolution's claims, and the validity of its findings.”
  • Playtech counter-attack (quote): “Evolution continues to seek to avoid legitimate scrutiny rather than addressing longstanding questions about its own conduct, including its decision to supply operators in both illegal and sanctioned markets and to support unlicensed operators in regulated markets.”
  • Evolution rebuttal: stated Playtech “currently operates or has operated in some of the very markets it accuses Evolution of operating in illegally,” and reaffirmed confidence in its compliance policies.

summary: Playtech denies Evolution's legal claims and welcomes court examination of disputed report
body:
Time Period Update on Evolution AB litigation step (referencing Evolution public statement on 21 October 2025)

  • Evolution AB stated it has requested permission from the Superior Court of New Jersey to add Playtech to Evolution’s ongoing legal case; Playtech says it had been “awaiting this development” since Evolution’s 21 October 2025 statement.
  • Playtech position: Evolution’s claims are “baseless and without merit.”
  • Playtech reiterates it stands by (i) the decision to commission the report at the centre of Evolution’s claims and (ii) the validity of the report’s findings.
  • Playtech escalates allegations toward Evolution: says Evolution is avoiding scrutiny and references questions about Evolution’s conduct, including supplying operators in illegal and sanctioned markets and supporting unlicensed operators in regulated markets.
  • Playtech welcomes court and regulatory examination and specifically highlights wanting to participate in discovery (including questioning Evolution employees/executives/officers in court).
  • Playtech: “The Company is very confident” (citing its evidence, including “recent additional evidence”) that proceedings will confirm the report’s credibility/legitimacy and the importance of the issues raised.
  • Playtech says it will defend itself vigorously and continue acting in the best interests of operators, suppliers, regulators, and shareholders.

summary: Shaftesbury Capital declares 2.1p final dividend per share, payable May 2026
body:
Time Period 2025 final cash dividend (payment timetable May 2026)

  • Confirms additional info for 2025 final cash dividend of 2.1p per share, to be paid wholly as a property income distribution (PID).
  • Dividend payment date: Friday, 22 May 2026; record date: Friday, 24 April 2026.
  • Dividend remains subject to shareholder approval at AGM on Thursday, 14 May 2026.
  • Declared ZAR exchange rate for the dividend: 22.0544 ZAR : 1 GBP, determined Wednesday, 8 April 2026.
  • Based on this, South African register holders receive 46.31424 ZAR cents gross per share; 37.05139 ZAR cents net of UK withholding tax.
  • UK withholding tax stated at 20% (unless exemptions apply): 0.42p (and 9.26285 ZAR cents).
  • PID net of UK withholding tax: 1.68p (and 37.05139 ZAR cents).
  • States there will be no Non-PID element of the proposed final dividend.
  • South African dividends tax discussion: mentions potential effective 5% SA Dividends Tax (where applicable) and a separate scenario where 20% SA Dividends Tax applies, with example net amounts 34.73568 ZAR cents (effective 5%) vs 27.78854 ZAR cents (20% SA rate), after UK withholding and SA Dividends Tax.
  • Notes SA shareholders may apply to HMRC after payment for a refund of the difference between 20% UK withholding and the UK/SA treaty rate of 15% (as described).

summary: Gina Jardine appointed Non-Executive Director of Resolute Mining from May 2026
body:
Time Period Appointment to take effect following Resolute Mining AGM on 20 May 2026

  • Gina Jardine (Forterra Non-Executive Director) will be appointed Non-Executive Director of Resolute Mining Limited (listed on LSE main market and ASX), following the AGM on 20 May 2026.

summary: SIX St Andrew Street fully leased to AI company at strong £200/sq ft rent
body:
Time Period Letting / leasing update as at 9 April 2026 (includes FY-end leasing momentum commentary)

  • Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street, EC4, completing leasing at the Fully Managed development (building now fully occupied).
  • The 3rd and 4th floors (total 11,680 sq ft) were let to an AI company.
  • Asset economics: now generating £8.8m annual rent at an average £200/sq ft.
  • Rent achieved is 6.2% ahead of ERV.
  • Reported 6.2% yield on cost for the building.
  • Portfolio mix shift: AI-related customers now represent 11.7% of GPE’s total office portfolio and 25.2% of its Fully Managed offices.
  • Fully Managed leasing momentum: activity “accelerated” in the final quarter with 19 deals completed and 7 further deals under offer, supported by “record” viewings and enquiries.
  • Building spec: 8 floors of refurbished Fully Managed offices (units 1,200–5,800 sq ft) with shared amenities (communal lounge/boardroom, rooftop terrace, wellness studio).
  • Quote (Molly Maguire, Senior Flex Leasing Manager): “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.”

summary: ME Group partners with ASDA to install up to 700 laundry machines across UK sites
body:
Time Period Partnership announcement; roll-out ambition and FY 2026 targets

  • Signed a new partnership with ASDA to install laundry machines across ASDA sites in the UK.
  • Scope: ME Group will install and operate machines at ASDA Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites.
  • Roll-out ambition: target up to 700 machines across ASDA sites (described as the “overall ambition”).
  • Current footprint: 7,600+ machines across 12 countries; 1,500+ located in the UK & Republic of Ireland.
  • Product spec highlights: self-service, large-capacity 9kg and 20kg drums; energy-efficient, high-speed wash/dry; available 24/7.
  • FY 2026 ambition: install more than 1,300 machines.
  • Long-term ambition: 20,000+ machines globally.
  • Stated 2025 delivery: “record” installations of 1,326 across geographies.
  • Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “ASDA is a perfect company to partner with… this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.”
  • Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “The rapid expansion of our laundry services is a key strategic priority… This partnership is indicative of our intention to further cement our UK market-leading position in the unattended laundry segment.”
  • Quote (Joseph Sutton, VP Asda Express, foodservice and fuel): “This large-scale rollout of units gives customers a quick, convenient and great-value way to tackle their laundry while they shop…”

  • Intel and Google announced a multiyear collaboration to advance AI / cloud infrastructure, explicitly reinforcing the role of CPUs + custom IPUs in “heterogeneous” AI systems.
  • Stated deployment: Google Cloud continues using Intel Xeon across workload-optimised instances; cites “latest Xeon 6” powering C4 and N4 instances (AI coordination, inference, general-purpose workloads).
  • Expansion area: co-development of custom ASIC-based infrastructure processing units (IPUs) to offload networking/storage/security from host CPUs to improve utilisation, efficiency and predictability at hyperscale.
  • Intel CEO quote: “AI is reshaping how infrastructure is built and scaled… Scaling AI requires more than accelerators - it requires balanced systems. CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand.”
  • Google (Amin Vahdat) quote: “CPUs and infrastructure acceleration remain a cornerstone of AI systems—from training orchestration to inference and deployment… Intel has been a trusted partner for nearly two decades, and their Xeon roadmap gives us confidence that we can continue to meet the growing performance and efficiency demands of our workloads.”

summary: PayPal Payment Links integrated into Canva enabling creators to accept payments directly from designs
body:
Time Period Product / partnership launch (no financial period)

  • PayPal Payment Links now available directly in Canva, positioned as enabling Canva’s “265 million monthly users worldwide” to convert designs into checkout experiences.
  • Value prop: creators can embed a PayPal-hosted checkout via payment link or QR code into digital/printed designs and accept payments “with no website required.”
  • Payment methods called out: PayPal checkout including PayPal, Venmo, and PayPal Pay Later (with the note: Venmo payments only available in the US; Pay Later in eligible markets).
  • Monetisation framing: points to social commerce (claims projected to surpass $1tn by 2028) and argues transactions are shifting “inside content, conversations, and communities.”
  • PayPal quote (Taira Hall): “By pairing PayPal's trusted global payment infrastructure with Canva's creative workflow, we're reducing the friction between inspiration and income and meeting them at point of need. With PayPal integrated directly in Canva, creators can move seamlessly from creating to getting paid.”
  • Canva quote (Emily MacDonald): “Whether someone's launching their first product, booking their next clients, or selling at a weekend market, having PayPal Payment Links right inside Canva means you can go from a bold idea to getting paid in just a few clicks, without ever leaving their design.”
  • Marketing/event: PayPal named “Official Payment Partner” of Canva Create on 16 April 2026 (Hollywood Park, Los Angeles); app available globally via Canva Marketplace.

Playtech PLC (and Evolution AB litigation update) Time Period US lawsuit / proposed defendant expansion (current)

  • Evolution says it has asked the Superior Court of New Jersey for permission to add Playtech as a defendant in its defamation suit (also against Calcagni & Kanefsky LLP and Black Cube).
  • Evolution allegation: Playtech hired Black Cube “to create and publicise a defamatory report designed to harm Evolution,” and that this amounted to a “smear campaign… to eliminate competition in the North American marketplace.”
  • Evolution says the filing accuses Playtech of “trade libel, fraud and racketeering” and alleges withholding information from shareholders; also alleges Playtech CEO Mor Weizer’s “active participation.”
  • Evolution claim on incentives: alleges Playtech promised Black Cube £1.5m in success fees.
  • Evolution claim re regulators: “Playtech and Black Cube's agreement outlined plans... to get regulators to open an investigation and ultimately revoke Evolution's gaming license.” It adds that the report “was determined by two state gaming regulators in the US to lack evidentiary support.”
  • Playtech response: called the claims “baseless and without merit,” adding: “Playtech stands by both the decision to commission the report at the centre of Evolution's claims, and the validity of its findings.”
  • Playtech counter-attack (quote): “Evolution continues to seek to avoid legitimate scrutiny rather than addressing longstanding questions about its own conduct, including its decision to supply operators in both illegal and sanctioned markets and to support unlicensed operators in regulated markets.”
  • Evolution rebuttal: stated Playtech “currently operates or has operated in some of the very markets it accuses Evolution of operating in illegally,” and reaffirmed confidence in its compliance policies.

summary: Playtech denies Evolution's litigation claims as baseless and welcomes court examination
body:
Time Period Update on Evolution AB litigation step (referencing Evolution public statement on 21 October 2025)

  • Evolution AB stated it has requested permission from the Superior Court of New Jersey to add Playtech to Evolution’s ongoing legal case; Playtech says it had been “awaiting this development” since Evolution’s 21 October 2025 statement.
  • Playtech position: Evolution’s claims are “baseless and without merit.”
  • Playtech reiterates it stands by (i) the decision to commission the report at the centre of Evolution’s claims and (ii) the validity of the report’s findings.
  • Playtech escalates allegations toward Evolution: says Evolution is avoiding scrutiny and references questions about Evolution’s conduct, including supplying operators in illegal and sanctioned markets and supporting unlicensed operators in regulated markets.
  • Playtech welcomes court and regulatory examination and specifically highlights wanting to participate in discovery (including questioning Evolution employees/executives/officers in court).
  • Playtech: “The Company is very confident” (citing its evidence, including “recent additional evidence”) that proceedings will confirm the report’s credibility/legitimacy and the importance of the issues raised.
  • Playtech says it will defend itself vigorously and continue acting in the best interests of operators, suppliers, regulators, and shareholders.

summary: Shaftesbury Capital declares 2.1p final dividend for 2025, payable May 2026
body:
Time Period 2025 final cash dividend (payment timetable May 2026)

  • Confirms additional info for 2025 final cash dividend of 2.1p per share, to be paid wholly as a property income distribution (PID).
  • Dividend payment date: Friday, 22 May 2026; record date: Friday, 24 April 2026.
  • Dividend remains subject to shareholder approval at AGM on Thursday, 14 May 2026.
  • Declared ZAR exchange rate for the dividend: 22.0544 ZAR : 1 GBP, determined Wednesday, 8 April 2026.
  • Based on this, South African register holders receive 46.31424 ZAR cents gross per share; 37.05139 ZAR cents net of UK withholding tax.
  • UK withholding tax stated at 20% (unless exemptions apply): 0.42p (and 9.26285 ZAR cents).
  • PID net of UK withholding tax: 1.68p (and 37.05139 ZAR cents).
  • States there will be no Non-PID element of the proposed final dividend.
  • South African dividends tax discussion: mentions potential effective 5% SA Dividends Tax (where applicable) and a separate scenario where 20% SA Dividends Tax applies, with example net amounts 34.73568 ZAR cents (effective 5%) vs 27.78854 ZAR cents (20% SA rate), after UK withholding and SA Dividends Tax.
  • Notes SA shareholders may apply to HMRC after payment for a refund of the difference between 20% UK withholding and the UK/SA treaty rate of 15% (as described).

summary: Gina Jardine appointed Non-Executive Director of Resolute Mining from May 2026
body:
Time Period Appointment to take effect following Resolute Mining AGM on 20 May 2026

  • Gina Jardine (Forterra Non-Executive Director) will be appointed Non-Executive Director of Resolute Mining Limited (listed on LSE main market and ASX), following the AGM on 20 May 2026.

summary: Great Portland Estates completes SIX St Andrew Street letting to AI company, achieving full occupancy
body:
Time Period Letting / leasing update as at 9 April 2026 (includes FY-end leasing momentum commentary)

  • Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street, EC4, completing leasing at the Fully Managed development (building now fully occupied).
  • The 3rd and 4th floors (total 11,680 sq ft) were let to an AI company.
  • Asset economics: now generating £8.8m annual rent at an average £200/sq ft.
  • Rent achieved is 6.2% ahead of ERV.
  • Reported 6.2% yield on cost for the building.
  • Portfolio mix shift: AI-related customers now represent 11.7% of GPE’s total office portfolio and 25.2% of its Fully Managed offices.
  • Fully Managed leasing momentum: activity “accelerated” in the final quarter with 19 deals completed and 7 further deals under offer, supported by “record” viewings and enquiries.
  • Building spec: 8 floors of refurbished Fully Managed offices (units 1,200–5,800 sq ft) with shared amenities (communal lounge/boardroom, rooftop terrace, wellness studio).
  • Quote (Molly Maguire, Senior Flex Leasing Manager): “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.”

summary: ME Group signs major ASDA partnership to install up to 700 laundry machines across UK sites
body:
Time Period Partnership announcement; roll-out ambition and FY 2026 targets

  • Signed a new partnership with ASDA to install laundry machines across ASDA sites in the UK.
  • Scope: ME Group will install and operate machines at ASDA Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites.
  • Roll-out ambition: target up to 700 machines across ASDA sites (described as the “overall ambition”).
  • Current footprint: 7,600+ machines across 12 countries; 1,500+ located in the UK & Republic of Ireland.
  • Product spec highlights: self-service, large-capacity 9kg and 20kg drums; energy-efficient, high-speed wash/dry; available 24/7.
  • FY 2026 ambition: install more than 1,300 machines.
  • Long-term ambition: 20,000+ machines globally.
  • Stated 2025 delivery: “record” installations of 1,326 across geographies.
  • Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “ASDA is a perfect company to partner with… this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.”
  • Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “The rapid expansion of our laundry services is a key strategic priority… This partnership is indicative of our intention to further cement our UK market-leading position in the unattended laundry segment.”
  • Quote (Joseph Sutton, VP Asda Express, foodservice and fuel): “This large-scale rollout of units gives customers a quick, convenient and great-value way to tackle their laundry while they shop…”

summary: Close Brothers estimates £320m FCA motor finance redress cost, manageable within capital resources
body:
Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme impact review (pro forma as at 31 January 2026; rollout summer 2026–end 2027)

  • Reviewed FCA Policy Statement PS26/3 (motor finance consumer redress scheme) and its impact; stated the published scheme’s estimated cost is c.£320m, “broadly similar” to existing provision and “can be comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources.
  • No provision changes recognised yet; existing IAS 37 provision remains under review.
  • Estimated scheme cost (if recognised in isolation) implies pro forma provision c.£320m as at 31 Jan 2026 vs IAS 37 provision £294m (difference +£26m / +8.8%).
  • Capital impact: would reduce CET1 ratio by c.25 bps to 14.0% (pro forma at 31 Jan 2026), still above medium-term target 12–13%.
  • Provision methodology: current £294m based on probability-weighted scenarios (including an implementation scenario aligned to the FCA’s 7 Oct 2025 consultation); the £320m estimate reflects the policy statement scenario only and changes relative to the consultation.
  • Key assumption: c.720k qualifying UK regulated motor finance loans written 6 Apr 2007–1 Nov 2024, including c.640k DCA loans + c.80k non-DCA loans likely meeting “tied” and/or “high commission” criteria.
  • Key assumption: average redress payment c.£500 incl. compensatory interest (vs industry average £829 cited by FCA), reflecting smaller loan sizes and lower commission levels in Close Brothers’ book.
  • Key assumption: 75% claim rate; each ±5% absolute change in claim rate drives ±c.£18m change in estimate (incl. delivery cost impact).
  • Key assumption: delivery cost c.£66m, excluding c.£14m of costs already incurred against the existing provision.
  • Key assumption: scheme rollout from summer 2026 to end-CY 2027, implying a time-value discount of c.£16m.
  • Footnote mechanics: the £294m balance sheet provision at 31 Jan 2026 reflects £300m charged to income statement, plus £8m partial unwind of discount (time value), less £14m costs incurred to date.

summary: Motorpoint reports record volumes of 65,000 units and 83% profit growth in FY26
body:
Time Period FY ended 31 March 2026 (FY26 trading update ahead of June 2026 results)

  • FY26 retail volumes c.65,000, +8.0% YoY (record volumes).
  • Profit before tax expected c.£7.5m, +83% YoY (FY25: £4.1m); attributed to record metal margin performance offsetting inflationary pressures.
  • EBITDA expected c.£27.0m, +13.0% YoY (FY25: £23.9m).
  • ROCE expected c.70%, positioned as evidence the “capital light model” is working.
  • Net Promoter Score improved to 83 (FY25: 80, +3 pts / +3.8%).
  • Outperformance vs used-car market: Motorpoint volumes grew 6.0% in calendar 2025 vs 2.2% for overall used-car market.
  • AI tooling: since launching an “agentic AI tool” to reactivate historic closed quotes, c.900 incremental vehicle sales attributed to that channel.
  • Supply/pricing: vehicle supply “more normalised”, enabling more bulk deals; increased sourcing via Sell Your Car channel; data-led buying/pricing described as embedded and supporting record metal margins (helping offset labour cost inflation).
  • New store: intends to open a Leeds store in Summer 2026.
  • CEO Mark Carpenter quote: “The Group had an excellent year in FY26, and I am delighted to report record sales volumes and an 83% increase in profit before taxation. FY26 has been a step change year for Motorpoint, where the use of data became fundamental within the business and we started to embrace the benefits of AI. Whilst the macroeconomic uncertainty in recent weeks leads to a degree of caution due to the risks of increased inflation and interest rates, our superior customer service, omnichannel business model and exciting growth plans mean we are well placed to take advantage of opportunities to further increase market share and build long term value for shareholders.”

summary: Universal Logistics appoints Michael Rogers as CFO effective June 1, 2026
body:
Time Period CFO transition effective 29 May–1 June 2026

  • Appointed Michael Rogers as Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, effective 1 June 2026.
  • Rogers’ current roles: CFO of Conlan Tire Co., Hercules Materials Holdings LLC, and certain privately held affiliates.
  • Rogers’ prior experience: ~30 years at Ford Motor Company in finance leadership roles (incl. Model e Controller – Product Development; Global Controller – Warranty; Global Controller – Material Planning & Logistics; Finance Director for Canada/Mexico/South America Ops).
  • Outgoing CFO: Jude M. Beres will resign as CFO & Treasurer effective 29 May 2026 (pursuing opportunities outside transportation/logistics) and is expected to stay through that date to support transition.
  • Quote (CEO Tim Phillips) on incoming CFO: “Mike brings tremendous breadth and depth of financial, operational and leadership experience… We believe Mike's background, judgment and familiarity with Universal will make him an outstanding addition to our senior leadership team."
  • Quote (CEO Tim Phillips) on outgoing CFO: “Jude has served as our Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer since 2016… Over the course of nearly three decades with the Company and its affiliated businesses, Jude has played an important role in our growth and development…”

summary: Microsoft will release FY 2026 Q3 earnings on April 29, 2026
body:
Time Period Fiscal year 2026 third quarter (earnings release scheduling)

  • Microsoft will publish FY 2026 Q3 financial results after market close on Wednesday, 29 April 2026.
  • Earnings conference call webcast scheduled for 2:30 p.m. Pacific Time.

summary: Tullow Oil achieves 97% consent for refinancing of $1.28 billion senior notes due 2026
body:
Time Period Refinancing / consent solicitation timeline (March–April 2026)

  • Announced that, as of 11pm (UK) 7 April 2026, holders representing >97% of the $1,285,245,000 10.25% Senior Secured Notes due 2026 provided consents for the refinancing-related amendments.
  • “Requisite Consents” threshold was 90% and was achieved on 7 April 2026.
  • Consents cover: (i) indenture + intercreditor amendments, (ii) release of existing Notes and issuance of New Notes under an amended/restated indenture, and (iii) waivers to permit release of collateral in connection with the refinancing.
  • Next steps: Tullow/trustee/others to execute a Second Supplemental Indenture (permit pro rata redemption, reduce minimum denominations, implement waiver) and a Third Supplemental Indenture (other proposed amendments); consents become non-revocable at the “Effective Time.”
  • Operability: Second Supplemental Indenture becomes effective/operative at Effective Time; Third becomes effective then, but certain amendments don’t become operative until settlement conditions are met.
  • Voting window remains open until 11:59pm (NYC) 21 April 2026 (unless extended/terminated/amended by Tullow).
  • Economic terms for all holders (per announcement):– 23 April 2026: portion of existing Notes redeemed at par, pro rata, plus accrued/unpaid interest on the redeemed amount.– 24 April 2026: receive (a) New Notes, (b) accrued/unpaid interest on remaining existing Notes then outstanding, and (c) a 1.00% fee calculated on aggregate principal outstanding as of the Expiration Time.

summary: Shell cuts Q1 2026 Integrated Gas production outlook citing Middle East conflict impacts
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 (outlook update; results scheduled 7 May 2026)

  • Updated Integrated Gas production outlook to 880–920 kboe/d, down from 948 kboe/d in Q4’25 (implies -7.2% to -3.0% vs Q4), citing impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes.
  • Cut Integrated Gas guidance versus prior range 920–980 kboe/d (low end -40 kboe/d / -4.3%, high end -60 kboe/d / -6.1%).
  • LNG liquefaction volumes guidance raised at the low end to 7.6–8.0 mt (from 7.4–8.0 mt, +0.2 mt / +2.7% at the low end); Q4’25 LNG volume was 7.8 mt.
  • LNG drivers: ramp-up of LNG Canada, offset by Australia weather constraints and Qatar LNG outages.
  • Flagged that, “In light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.”
  • Upstream production guidance: 1,760–1,860 kboe/d, down from 1,892 kboe/d in Q4’25 (implies -7.0% to -1.7% vs Q4), including reduced production following Adura JV incorporation.
  • Indicative refining margin: $17/bbl (Q4’25: $14/bbl, +21.4%).
  • Trading & optimisation: expected to be significantly higher than Q4’25 in Chemicals & Products and Renewables & Energy Solutions; Integrated Gas trading & optimisation expected in line with Q4’25.
  • Working capital expected to swing to -$10bn to -$15bn in Q1’26 vs +$1.3bn in Q4’25 (deterioration of ~$11.3bn to $16.3bn), attributed to “unprecedented volatility in commodity prices on inventory and receivables.”
  • Non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases” in the current macro environment.

summary: CME Group reports record Q1 2026 international volumes, up 30% year-over-year
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 (international volume statistics)

  • International average daily volume (ADV) reached a record 11.4m contracts in Q1 2026, +30% YoY.
  • Record international interest rate ADV of 5.7m contracts, +30% YoY.
  • Other international asset-class quarterly records: metals +116%, energy +62%, agricultural +16%, equity index +11%, FX +1% (YoY).
  • Regional highlights: EMEA ADV 8.4m (+29% YoY); APAC ADV 2.6m (+33% YoY); LatAm ADV 224k (+21% YoY); Canada ADV 219k (+12% YoY).
  • Global ADV (all markets) reported at 36.2m contracts in Q1 2026, +22% YoY; stated this was also the first time quarterly volumes hit record levels across rates, energy, metals, equity index, agriculture and FX.
  • Julie Winkler (Senior Managing Director & Chief Commercial Officer) quote: “This surge in trading activity demonstrates how our global client base is turning to CME Group to manage risk in real time, through our benchmark products and on a regulated marketplace… In this risk-always-on environment, our deeply liquid markets are critical for helping clients hedge their exposure and pursue opportunities across all asset classes and time zones.”

summary: Greatland Resources delivered strong Q3 FY26 production and cash position ahead of guidance
body:
Time Period March 2026 quarter (FY26 YTD also referenced)

  • Quarter production: 82,723 oz Au and 4,128t Cu (FY26 YTD: 249,887 oz Au, 11,022t Cu).
  • Quarter sales: 97,800 oz Au and 4,620t Cu (FY26 YTD sales: 252,212 oz Au, 10,333t Cu).
  • Quarter-end cash: $1,208m at 31 March 2026 vs $948m at 31 Dec 2025 (+$260m / +27.4% QoQ), with nil debt; cash build stated after capex and after a $73m tax payment relating to FY25.
  • Will commence paying regular cash tax instalments from April 2026.
  • Still to report March-quarter AISC (to be finalised in the March 2026 Quarterly Activities Report).
  • Hedging posture: maintains “full upside exposure to the gold price” with “partial downside price protection” via gold put options.
  • Middle East conflict monitoring: says Telfer is not currently impacted by diesel supply disruptions (fuel supplied directly by a global oil major on a long-term contract via Port Hedland); power generated onsite by natural gas; underground operation uses an electric shaft hoist to reduce diesel intensity.
  • Surface stockpiles: 22Mt @ 0.36g/t Au and 0.05% Cu at end-March, stated as >12 months of mill feed.
  • FY26 production expectation: “around, or slightly above, the upper end” of guidance range 260,000–310,000 oz Au.
  • Full March 2026 Quarterly Activities Report expected later in April 2026; webcast to be held the same day (details “in due course”).

summary: Capricorn Energy extends PUSU deadline to May 6 for potential cash offer from Cafani Group
body:
Time Period Offer period / PUSU timetable update (possible offer)

  • Announced extension of the PUSU deadline under Rule 2.6 of the Takeover Code relating to a potential all-cash offer by Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC (Cafani Group), following multiple unsolicited non-binding proposals (first disclosed 11 March 2026).
  • Original PUSU deadline: 5:00pm (London) on 8 April 2026; extended (with Takeover Panel consent) to 5:00pm on 6 May 2026 to allow more time for the bidder to progress funding arrangements.
  • Stated the revised deadline can be further extended with Panel consent.
  • Repeated standard Takeover Code language: no certainty any firm offer will be made or on what terms; shareholders advised to take no action.
  • Company remains in an “Offer Period” (so dealing disclosure requirements continue to apply).

  • Economic terms for all holders (per announcement):– 23 April 2026: portion of existing Notes redeemed at par, pro rata, plus accrued/unpaid interest on the redeemed amount.– 24 April 2026: receive (a) New Notes, (b) accrued/unpaid interest on remaining existing Notes then outstanding, and (c) a 1.00% fee calculated on aggregate principal outstanding as of the Expiration Time.

summary: Shell cuts Q1 2026 gas production outlook due to Middle East conflict impacts
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 (outlook update; results scheduled 7 May 2026)

  • Updated Integrated Gas production outlook to 880–920 kboe/d, down from 948 kboe/d in Q4’25 (implies -7.2% to -3.0% vs Q4), citing impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes.
  • Cut Integrated Gas guidance versus prior range 920–980 kboe/d (low end -40 kboe/d / -4.3%, high end -60 kboe/d / -6.1%).
  • LNG liquefaction volumes guidance raised at the low end to 7.6–8.0 mt (from 7.4–8.0 mt, +0.2 mt / +2.7% at the low end); Q4’25 LNG volume was 7.8 mt.
  • LNG drivers: ramp-up of LNG Canada, offset by Australia weather constraints and Qatar LNG outages.
  • Flagged that, “In light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.”
  • Upstream production guidance: 1,760–1,860 kboe/d, down from 1,892 kboe/d in Q4’25 (implies -7.0% to -1.7% vs Q4), including reduced production following Adura JV incorporation.
  • Indicative refining margin: $17/bbl (Q4’25: $14/bbl, +21.4%).
  • Trading & optimisation: expected to be significantly higher than Q4’25 in Chemicals & Products and Renewables & Energy Solutions; Integrated Gas trading & optimisation expected in line with Q4’25.
  • Working capital expected to swing to -$10bn to -$15bn in Q1’26 vs +$1.3bn in Q4’25 (deterioration of ~$11.3bn to $16.3bn), attributed to “unprecedented volatility in commodity prices on inventory and receivables.”
  • Non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases” in the current macro environment.

summary: CME Group's Q1 2026 international volumes surged 30% to record 11.4m contracts daily
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 (international volume statistics)

  • International average daily volume (ADV) reached a record 11.4m contracts in Q1 2026, +30% YoY.
  • Record international interest rate ADV of 5.7m contracts, +30% YoY.
  • Other international asset-class quarterly records: metals +116%, energy +62%, agricultural +16%, equity index +11%, FX +1% (YoY).
  • Regional highlights: EMEA ADV 8.4m (+29% YoY); APAC ADV 2.6m (+33% YoY); LatAm ADV 224k (+21% YoY); Canada ADV 219k (+12% YoY).
  • Global ADV (all markets) reported at 36.2m contracts in Q1 2026, +22% YoY; stated this was also the first time quarterly volumes hit record levels across rates, energy, metals, equity index, agriculture and FX.
  • Julie Winkler (Senior Managing Director & Chief Commercial Officer) quote: “This surge in trading activity demonstrates how our global client base is turning to CME Group to manage risk in real time, through our benchmark products and on a regulated marketplace… In this risk-always-on environment, our deeply liquid markets are critical for helping clients hedge their exposure and pursue opportunities across all asset classes and time zones.”

summary: Greatland Resources reports strong Q3 FY26 cash position of $1.208bn with nil debt
body:
Time Period March 2026 quarter (FY26 YTD also referenced)

  • Quarter production: 82,723 oz Au and 4,128t Cu (FY26 YTD: 249,887 oz Au, 11,022t Cu).
  • Quarter sales: 97,800 oz Au and 4,620t Cu (FY26 YTD sales: 252,212 oz Au, 10,333t Cu).
  • Quarter-end cash: $1,208m at 31 March 2026 vs $948m at 31 Dec 2025 (+$260m / +27.4% QoQ), with nil debt; cash build stated after capex and after a $73m tax payment relating to FY25.
  • Will commence paying regular cash tax instalments from April 2026.
  • Still to report March-quarter AISC (to be finalised in the March 2026 Quarterly Activities Report).
  • Hedging posture: maintains “full upside exposure to the gold price” with “partial downside price protection” via gold put options.
  • Middle East conflict monitoring: says Telfer is not currently impacted by diesel supply disruptions (fuel supplied directly by a global oil major on a long-term contract via Port Hedland); power generated onsite by natural gas; underground operation uses an electric shaft hoist to reduce diesel intensity.
  • Surface stockpiles: 22Mt @ 0.36g/t Au and 0.05% Cu at end-March, stated as >12 months of mill feed.
  • FY26 production expectation: “around, or slightly above, the upper end” of guidance range 260,000–310,000 oz Au.
  • Full March 2026 Quarterly Activities Report expected later in April 2026; webcast to be held the same day (details “in due course”).

summary: Capricorn Energy extends PUSU deadline to May 6 for potential cash offer from Cafani Group
body:
Time Period Offer period / PUSU timetable update (possible offer)

  • Announced extension of the PUSU deadline under Rule 2.6 of the Takeover Code relating to a potential all-cash offer by Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC (Cafani Group), following multiple unsolicited non-binding proposals (first disclosed 11 March 2026).
  • Original PUSU deadline: 5:00pm (London) on 8 April 2026; extended (with Takeover Panel consent) to 5:00pm on 6 May 2026 to allow more time for the bidder to progress funding arrangements.
  • Stated the revised deadline can be further extended with Panel consent.
  • Repeated standard Takeover Code language: no certainty any firm offer will be made or on what terms; shareholders advised to take no action.
  • Company remains in an “Offer Period” (so dealing disclosure requirements continue to apply).

summary: Motorpoint reports record volumes of 65,000 units with profit up 83% to £7.5m
body:
Time Period FY ended 31 March 2026 (FY26 trading update ahead of June 2026 results)

  • FY26 retail volumes c.65,000, +8.0% YoY (record volumes).
  • Profit before tax expected c.£7.5m, +83% YoY (FY25: £4.1m); attributed to record metal margin performance offsetting inflationary pressures.
  • EBITDA expected c.£27.0m, +13.0% YoY (FY25: £23.9m).
  • ROCE expected c.70%, positioned as evidence the “capital light model” is working.
  • Net Promoter Score improved to 83 (FY25: 80, +3 pts / +3.8%).
  • Outperformance vs used-car market: Motorpoint volumes grew 6.0% in calendar 2025 vs 2.2% for overall used-car market.
  • AI tooling: since launching an “agentic AI tool” to reactivate historic closed quotes, c.900 incremental vehicle sales attributed to that channel.
  • Supply/pricing: vehicle supply “more normalised”, enabling more bulk deals; increased sourcing via Sell Your Car channel; data-led buying/pricing described as embedded and supporting record metal margins (helping offset labour cost inflation).
  • New store: intends to open a Leeds store in Summer 2026.
  • CEO Mark Carpenter quote: “The Group had an excellent year in FY26, and I am delighted to report record sales volumes and an 83% increase in profit before taxation. FY26 has been a step change year for Motorpoint, where the use of data became fundamental within the business and we started to embrace the benefits of AI. Whilst the macroeconomic uncertainty in recent weeks leads to a degree of caution due to the risks of increased inflation and interest rates, our superior customer service, omnichannel business model and exciting growth plans mean we are well placed to take advantage of opportunities to further increase market share and build long term value for shareholders.”

summary: Close Brothers estimates £320m FCA motor finance redress cost, manageable within capital resources
body:
Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme impact review (pro forma as at 31 January 2026; rollout summer 2026–end 2027)

  • Reviewed FCA Policy Statement PS26/3 (motor finance consumer redress scheme) and its impact; stated the published scheme’s estimated cost is c.£320m, “broadly similar” to existing provision and “can be comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources.
  • No provision changes recognised yet; existing IAS 37 provision remains under review.
  • Estimated scheme cost (if recognised in isolation) implies pro forma provision c.£320m as at 31 Jan 2026 vs IAS 37 provision £294m (difference +£26m / +8.8%).
  • Capital impact: would reduce CET1 ratio by c.25 bps to 14.0% (pro forma at 31 Jan 2026), still above medium-term target 12–13%.
  • Provision methodology: current £294m based on probability-weighted scenarios (including an implementation scenario aligned to the FCA’s 7 Oct 2025 consultation); the £320m estimate reflects the policy statement scenario only and changes relative to the consultation.
  • Key assumption: c.720k qualifying UK regulated motor finance loans written 6 Apr 2007–1 Nov 2024, including c.640k DCA loans + c.80k non-DCA loans likely meeting “tied” and/or “high commission” criteria.
  • Key assumption: average redress payment c.£500 incl. compensatory interest (vs industry average £829 cited by FCA), reflecting smaller loan sizes and lower commission levels in Close Brothers’ book.
  • Key assumption: 75% claim rate; each ±5% absolute change in claim rate drives ±c.£18m change in estimate (incl. delivery cost impact).
  • Key assumption: delivery cost c.£66m, excluding c.£14m of costs already incurred against the existing provision.
  • Key assumption: scheme rollout from summer 2026 to end-CY 2027, implying a time-value discount of c.£16m.
  • Footnote mechanics: the £294m balance sheet provision at 31 Jan 2026 reflects £300m charged to income statement, plus £8m partial unwind of discount (time value), less £14m costs incurred to date.

summary: Universal Logistics appoints Michael Rogers as new CFO effective June 1, 2026
body:
Time Period CFO transition effective 29 May–1 June 2026

  • Appointed Michael Rogers as Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, effective 1 June 2026.
  • Rogers’ current roles: CFO of Conlan Tire Co., Hercules Materials Holdings LLC, and certain privately held affiliates.
  • Rogers’ prior experience: ~30 years at Ford Motor Company in finance leadership roles (incl. Model e Controller – Product Development; Global Controller – Warranty; Global Controller – Material Planning & Logistics; Finance Director for Canada/Mexico/South America Ops).
  • Outgoing CFO: Jude M. Beres will resign as CFO & Treasurer effective 29 May 2026 (pursuing opportunities outside transportation/logistics) and is expected to stay through that date to support transition.
  • Quote (CEO Tim Phillips) on incoming CFO: “Mike brings tremendous breadth and depth of financial, operational and leadership experience… We believe Mike's background, judgment and familiarity with Universal will make him an outstanding addition to our senior leadership team."
  • Quote (CEO Tim Phillips) on outgoing CFO: “Jude has served as our Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer since 2016… Over the course of nearly three decades with the Company and its affiliated businesses, Jude has played an important role in our growth and development…”

summary: Microsoft will release FY2026 Q3 earnings on April 29, 2026 after market close
body:
Time Period Fiscal year 2026 third quarter (earnings release scheduling)

  • Microsoft will publish FY 2026 Q3 financial results after market close on Wednesday, 29 April 2026.
  • Earnings conference call webcast scheduled for 2:30 p.m. Pacific Time.

summary: Hunting PLC wins USD63.5 million Guyana offshore orders through May 2028
body:
Time Period Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026

  • Won multiple orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana.
  • Award value: USD63.5 million.
  • Delivery schedule: through May 2028.
  • Revenue recognition expected to start in H2 2026.
  • Work will be completed by the Subsea Spring business unit.
  • These awards are in addition to USD4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development by Hunting’s Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions business units since end-2025.
  • Hunting expects further incremental orders for this Guyana development “throughout the remainder of 2026”.
  • Hunting frames the awards as validating its TSJ solution on FPSO developments, citing “maintenance benefits and reliability”.
  • CEO Jim Johnson said: “Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered.”
  • CEO Jim Johnson said: “This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and [earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortisation] through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026.”

summary: Caledonia Mining reports better-than-expected deep drilling results at Zimbabwe's Blanket mine
body:
Time Period Deep level drilling programme (March–December 2025); resource/reserve update expected during 2026

  • Reported better-than-expected results from its deep level drilling programme at the Blanket mine (Zimbabwe).
  • Stated the orebodies returned grades and widths consistent with or exceeding expectations, prompting an intent to update the mineral resource estimate during 2026.
  • Said the results suggest gold mineralisation may extend deeper than previously anticipated.
  • Market reaction noted: shares up 5.7% to 1,850p (Tuesday morning, London).
  • Completed 10,312 metres of deep level drilling between March and December 2025.
  • Eroica orebody (≈1 km north of Blanket): results consistent with expectations.
  • Blanket orebody: identified a new intercept with “excellent” grades up to 9.2 g/t at 1,045m depth.
  • Newly discovered orebody has been named “Blanket 7”.
  • Lima orebody: confirmed to “34 level”, meaning gold present at 1,110m depth; Caledonia says this supports potential for further depth extensions.
  • Said deep drilling results will be incorporated into an updated mineral resource and mineral reserve statement to be released this year.
  • CEO said: “The latest results from our deep drilling programme reinforce the geological strength of Blanket mine and demonstrate the continuity of mineralisation at depth across multiple orebodies. The consistency of grades and widths we are seeing...provides growing confidence in the scale and quality of the mineral resource below the current lowest levels of the mine.”
  • CEO said: “These outcomes support our longer term planning efforts and highlight the value of the investments we have made”.

summary: Wickes Group announces £10m share buyback programme running April to December 2026
body:
Time Period Share buyback programme from 07 April 2026 to on/before 31 December 2026

  • Announced commencement of a third share buyback programme (the “2026 Programme”) following its FY results release on 17 March 2026.
  • Maximum aggregate market value to be bought back: £10,000,000 (excluding associated costs and stamp duty).
  • Programme start date: 07 April 2026.
  • Programme end date: on or before 31 December 2026.
  • Stated sole purpose: reduce share capital.
  • Company intends to cancel any shares purchased.
  • Entered into non-discretionary instructions with Investec Bank plc to conduct the programme.
  • Investec will act as principal and make trading decisions independently of Wickes.
  • Programme is within existing AGM authority, which at the date of announcement equals 16,502,960 ordinary shares.
  • Said the maximum aggregate number of shares purchasable will be updated to reflect authority granted at the 2026 AGM (19 May 2026).
  • Programme to be conducted in accordance with Market Abuse Regulation (596/2014) (as retained in UK law) and the FCA Listing Rules.

summary: Robert Perrins bought 14,000 Berkeley Group shares worth £424,025 across two transactions
body:
Time Period Director dealing disclosures covering purchases on 07 April 2026 and 01 April 2026

  • Executive Chair Robert Perrins bought 7,000 shares at £31.79 each (total £222,543) in London on 07 April 2026.
  • This follows a prior purchase of 7,000 shares at £28.78 each (total £201,482) on 01 April 2026.
  • The later purchase price (£31.79) is £3.01 higher than the earlier purchase price (£28.78), a +10.5% increase.
  • Combined across the two disclosed purchases: 14,000 shares acquired for £424,025 (total consideration).
  • The purchases were reported as occurring after Berkeley shares fell following the company’s decision to halt land buying, framed as linked to a UK housing market downturn attributed to the conflict in the Middle East.

summary: Peel Hunt downgraded Antofagasta to reduce citing weak Q1 production and cut price target
body:
Time Period Q1 2026 outlook and FY 2026 forecast revisions

  • Broker Peel Hunt moved its rating to “reduce” (sell) and cut its price target to 3,155p from 3,285p (-4.0%).
  • Peel Hunt relayed management’s view that Q1 is likely to be the “softest” quarter for production in 2026.
  • Peel Hunt reduced its Q1 copper production forecast to 136,000 tonnes.
  • Peel Hunt forecasts Q1 production down 12% year-on-year.
  • Peel Hunt forecasts Q1 production down 23% quarter-on-quarter.
  • Peel Hunt said the “net effect” is a 29,000 tonne reduction to its 2026 production forecast, now 642,000 tonnes.
  • Peel Hunt cut its FY 2026 revenue estimate by 5% to USD8.8 billion.
  • Peel Hunt cut its 2026 adjusted EBITDA estimate by 8% to USD5.3 billion.
  • Peel Hunt said: “the ‘lower output impacts revenue directly’”.
  • Peel Hunt said its base case implies the shares trade at “early 10x 2026 adjusted Ebitda”, which it described as “more typical of trough-cycle valuations rather than a period of record copper prices.”
  • Peel Hunt warned: “Another year of copper output near the bottom of the guidance range, or a further cut to guidance, could continue to test investor patience and result in further share price underperformance,”

summary: Barclays to redeem A$250 million floating rate debt instruments on 20 May 2026
body:
Time Period Early redemption on 20 May 2026; interest accrual 20 Feb 2026 to (excluding) 20 May 2026; instrument originally due May 2027

  • Announced notice of redemption for A$250,000,000 Floating Rate Debt Instruments due May 2027 (ISIN: AU3FN0060448).
  • Will fully redeem all outstanding instruments on the interest payment date falling on or immediately after 20 May 2026 (the “Early Redemption Date (Call)”).
  • Redemption price: 100% of principal plus accrued but unpaid interest.
  • Accrued interest period specified: from (and including) 20 February 2026 to (but excluding) 20 May 2026.
  • Payment date for the redemption amount: 20 May 2026.
  • Stated that the LSE International Securities Market listing of the instruments will be cancelled on, or shortly after, 21 May 2026.
  • Redemption is stated to be in accordance with Condition 10.4 of the instruments’ terms and conditions.
  • Referenced documentation: the company’s Supplement dated 18 May 2021 and Information Memorandum dated 7 May 2021 (as defining the relevant terms/conditions).
  • Provided contact for queries: Barclays Debt Investor Relations, +44 20 7116 1000, 1 Churchill Place, London E14 5HP.

summary: Sylvania Platinum repurchased 80,000 shares at 92.88 pence, increasing treasury holdings
body:
Time Period Share repurchase executed 02 April 2026 (announced 07 April 2026)

  • Acquired 80,000 ordinary shares on 02 April 2026 at an average price of 92.88 pence per share.
  • Shares purchased were placed into Treasury.
  • Implied cash consideration for the shares (excluding fees/expenses) is ~GBP74,304.
  • Issued share capital stated as 271,661,725 ordinary shares.
  • Total treasury shares following the purchase: 12,787,534.
  • Treasury shares are ~4.71% of issued share capital.
  • Total shares with voting rights following the purchase: 258,874,191.

summary: Leo Quinn becomes Executive Chair of WH Smith from April 7, 2026
body:
Time Period Board changes effective 07 April 2026 (following GM on 12 March 2026)

  • Following shareholder approval at the General Meeting on 12 March 2026, Leo Quinn assumes the role of Executive Chair with effect from 07 April 2026.
  • Andrew Harrison steps down from the Board with immediate effect.
  • Andrew Harrison resumes his role as CEO of the Group’s UK division.
  • Leo Quinn is described as the former CEO of Balfour Beatty PLC.

summary: Deep drilling at Blanket Mine confirms better-than-expected grades supporting increased mineral resource confidence
body:
Time Period Deep level drilling completed March–December 2025; targets resource confidence/extension beyond 34 level (1,110m depth)

  • Reports “better than expected grades and widths” from deep level drilling at Blanket Mine, supporting increased confidence in the overall mineral resource.
  • Deep drilling campaign completed 10,311.9 metres between March and December 2025.
  • Drilling objective: assess continuity of mineralised zones on the Blanket, Eroica and Lima orebodies to increase confidence in existing mineral resources.
  • Targeted growing the mineral resource estimate beyond 34 level, stated as 1,110 metres below surface.
  • States Blanket and Eroica orebodies returned grades/widths consistent with, or better than, expectations.
  • Notes multiple wide zones within newly identified Blanket 7 (BLK7) orebody as part of the “high grade intersections at depth” theme.
  • Says Lima orebody confirmed to 34 level, supporting potential depth extensions.
  • Expects results to increase mineral resource confidence and support future life-of-mine planning.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2401): 13.02m true width at 9.24 g/t (intersection depth from surface 1045.3m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2402): 40.42m true width at 3.14 g/t (depth 1075.0m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2402, included interval): 6.38m true width at 9.90 g/t.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2403): 46.28m true width at 4.25 g/t (depth 1082.0m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2403, included interval): 1.16m true width at 53.39 g/t.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2404): 20.50m true width at 3.98 g/t (depth 1054.1m).
  • BQRHWN (ARS1110EX2503): 3.65m true width at 7.74 g/t (depth 1099.3m).
  • BQR (BLK1110EX2412): 7.17m true width at 7.15 g/t (depth 1083.0m).
  • ERCN_HW (ERC750EX2404): 10.80m true width at 6.54 g/t (depth 994.3m).
  • ERCN_HW (ERC750EX2404, included interval): 1.54m true width at 14.20 g/t.
  • LIMM (LIM750EX2509): 1.98m true width at 15.33 g/t (depth 708.5m).

summary: Hunting secures USD63.5 million Guyana offshore orders through May 2028
body:
Time Period Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026

  • Won multiple orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana.
  • Award value: USD63.5 million.
  • Delivery schedule: through May 2028.
  • Revenue recognition expected to start in H2 2026.
  • Work will be completed by the Subsea Spring business unit.

  • States Blanket and Eroica orebodies returned grades/widths consistent with, or better than, expectations.
  • Notes multiple wide zones within newly identified Blanket 7 (BLK7) orebody as part of the “high grade intersections at depth” theme.
  • Says Lima orebody confirmed to 34 level, supporting potential depth extensions.
  • Expects results to increase mineral resource confidence and support future life-of-mine planning.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2401): 13.02m true width at 9.24 g/t (intersection depth from surface 1045.3m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2402): 40.42m true width at 3.14 g/t (depth 1075.0m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2402, included interval): 6.38m true width at 9.90 g/t.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2403): 46.28m true width at 4.25 g/t (depth 1082.0m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2403, included interval): 1.16m true width at 53.39 g/t.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2404): 20.50m true width at 3.98 g/t (depth 1054.1m).
  • BQRHWN (ARS1110EX2503): 3.65m true width at 7.74 g/t (depth 1099.3m).
  • BQR (BLK1110EX2412): 7.17m true width at 7.15 g/t (depth 1083.0m).
  • ERCN_HW (ERC750EX2404): 10.80m true width at 6.54 g/t (depth 994.3m).
  • ERCN_HW (ERC750EX2404, included interval): 1.54m true width at 14.20 g/t.
  • LIMM (LIM750EX2509): 1.98m true width at 15.33 g/t (depth 708.5m).

summary: Carol Howle appointed Deputy CEO effective April 2026, overseeing portfolio review and strategy
body:
Time Period Management appointments effective 02 April 2026; strategy work referenced “beyond” 2027 targets

  • Appointed Carol Howle as Deputy Chief Executive Officer, effective 02 April 2026.
  • Howle will continue to lead Supply, Trading & Shipping.
  • In the new role, Howle will oversee BP’s ongoing portfolio review and strategic development.
  • BP notes Howle has spent 25 years with the company.
  • BP says Howle served as interim CEO in December 2025.
  • Meg O’Neill (described as CEO since 01 April 2026) said: “As I step into my new role I am committed to providing clear direction and consistency so our company can move forward with confidence.”
  • Meg O’Neill said: “To support this, Carol will oversee the ongoing portfolio review and long-term strategy development beyond our 2027 targets. I'm looking forward to working closely with her to deliver a simpler, stronger, more valuable BP to maximize value for our shareholders.”

summary: Wizz Air has 103.5 million shares issued with potential 23.5% dilution from convertibles
body:
Time Period As at 31 March 2026

  • Share capital (one class): 103,461,185 ordinary shares in issue (par value £0.0001 each); no treasury shares.
  • Total voting rights denominator: 103,461,185.
  • Voting rights caveat: shares held by Non-Qualifying Nationals subject to proportional disenfranchisement (most recently referenced by the company on 25 June 2025).
  • “Theoretical fully diluted” share capital stated at 127,769,507, comprising:

  • Implied incremental dilution versus current issued shares if fully diluted: +24,308,322 shares (i.e. ~23.5% uplift vs current issued share count).

summary: Wizz Air grew passengers 10% YoY while expanding Italian operations and reopening Eastern European bases
body:
Time Period March 2026 (and rolling 12 months to March 2026)

  • March passengers: 5.51m vs 5.08m (+8.4% YoY).
  • March capacity (seats): 6.12m vs 5.61m (+9.1% YoY).
  • March load factor: 90.0% vs 90.5% (-0.5 ppts YoY).
  • Rolling 12-month passengers: 69.75m vs 63.40m (+10.0% YoY).
  • Rolling 12-month capacity (seats): 76.88m vs 69.55m (+10.5% YoY).
  • Rolling 12-month load factor: 90.7% vs 91.2% (-0.4 ppts YoY).
  • Operational note: delivered March growth “despite Middle East cuts” (no further quantification given).
  • Base update: Târgu Mureș base reopened 31 March with one A320neo; now 10 routes to 8 countries (examples listed include Milan Bergamo, Rome Fiumicino, Brussels Charleroi, Paris Beauvais, Dortmund, Memmingen, London Luton).
  • Base update: Podgorica base opened 30 March; now 17 international routes from the capital (positioned as making Montenegro a “key market”).
  • Italy fleet allocation: confirmed 10th based aircraft at Milan Malpensa and 17th at Rome Fiumicino (both from October 2026).
  • Italy fleet allocation: confirmed 4th based aircraft at Venice from September 2026.
  • Italy market share: originating seats market share ~11% for FY ending March 2026, ranked #2 overall (per Innovata + Wizz data).
  • New ancillary stream: rolled out Bluetooth-enabled in-seat ordering across the fleet via the mobile app (browse/purchase onboard even without internet access).
  • March CO2 emissions: 463,585 MT vs 467,497 MT (-0.8% YoY).
  • March CO2 intensity: 49.6 g/pax/km vs 51.0 (-2.7% YoY).
  • Rolling 12-month CO2 emissions: 6,060,538 MT vs 5,796,439 MT (+4.6% YoY).
  • Rolling 12-month CO2 intensity: 50.6 g/pax/km vs 52.2 (-3.1% YoY).

summary: Lloyds maintains existing motor finance redress provision following FCA scheme rules
body:
Time Period N/A (regulatory/provisioning update)

  • Following the FCA’s publication of final rules for an industry-wide motor finance redress scheme, Lloyds says it has assessed implications and impact and: “does not currently believe any change to the provision for this issue is required.” ()
  • Lloyds flags ongoing uncertainties that could change the ultimate cost: response rates, operational costs, litigation, and “potential actions by various parties, including legal proceedings and complaints.”
  • Lloyds: “An update will be provided as appropriate with the first quarter results at the end of April.”
  • Lloyds: “The Group remains committed to ensuring customers receive appropriate and timely redress.”
  • External context on the FCA scheme (for sizing): FCA indicates the scheme covers motor finance agreements in the 2007–2024 period and expects ~12.1m agreements to be in-scope, with average redress around ~£830 per agreement and total compensation estimated around ~£7.5bn (with broader industry cost estimates higher once costs are included). ()

summary: BHP receives $4.3bn upfront from Wheaton for Antamina silver streaming agreement
body:
Time Period Transaction completion (effective 01 April 2026)

  • Completed its long-term silver streaming transaction with Wheaton Precious Metals covering BHP’s share of silver production from the Antamina Mine (Peru). ()
  • BHP received US$4.3bn total upfront consideration.
  • In exchange, BHP will transfer to Wheaton the equivalent of 33.75% of Antamina silver production (subject to a fixed payable rate of 90%), plus ongoing production transfer payments set at 20% of the spot silver price per ounce delivered under the agreement. ()
  • After 100 million ounces have been delivered to Wheaton, the stream reduces to 22.5% of Antamina silver production for the remaining life of mine.
  • Settlement is via metal credits (i.e. no physical delivery of silver to Wheaton).

summary: Cairn Homes allots 3.4m shares for employee incentive plan vesting
body:
Time Period N/A (equity issue announcement)

  • Approved allotment of 3,426,403 new ordinary shares (€0.001 each) to satisfy vesting of employee share awards under the Cairn Homes plc Long Term Incentive Plan.
  • Application made for the new shares to be admitted to trading on Euronext Dublin and the UK Official List; admission expected 07 April 2026.
  • New shares will rank pari passu with existing shares.
  • Total issued share capital increases to 629,002,525 ordinary shares (implies pre-allotment shares of 625,576,122), i.e. share count up ~0.55%.

summary: BHP completes $4.3bn silver streaming agreement with Wheaton from Antamina
body:
Time Period Transaction completion (effective date 1 April 2026)

  • BHP confirmed it has completed the long-term silver streaming agreement with Wheaton (following the earlier announcement on 17 Feb 2026). ()
  • BHP has received the US$4.3bn upfront consideration from Wheaton. ()
  • In return, BHP will transfer exposure equivalent to 33.75% of Antamina silver production (subject to 90% payable rate) until 100Moz delivered; thereafter stream reduces to 22.5%. ()
  • Ongoing production transfer payments are 20% of spot silver price per ounce delivered. ()
  • Mechanics: “The settlement of the stream is completed via metal credits with no physical delivery of silver to Wheaton.” ()
